tldr: 将指代图像分割任务转化为顺序多边形生成，提出PolyFormer框架实现文本引导的精确分割。
motivation: 传统像素级分割存在精度限制，多边形生成更符合人类标注习惯，且可避免坐标量化误差。
method: 基于序列到序列的PolyFormer框架，输入图像块和文本查询，输出多边形顶点序列，采用回归解码器直接预测浮点坐标。
result: 在RefCOCO+和RefCOCOg数据集上分别提升5.40%和4.52%，视频分割任务达到61.5% J&F指标。
conclusion: PolyFormer通过多边形生成有效提升分割精度，具有良好的泛化能力，适用于视频分割等任务。
search_text: PolyFormer: Referring Image Segmentation as Sequential Polygon Generation PolyFormer：将图像分割称为顺序多边形生成 In this work, instead of directly predicting the pixel-level segmentation masks, the problem of referring image segmentation is formulated as sequential polygon generation, and the predicted polygons can be later converted into segmentation masks. This is enabled by a new sequence-to-sequence framework, Polygon Transformer (PolyFormer), which takes a sequence of image patches and text query tokens as input, and outputs a sequence of polygon vertices autoregressively. For more accurate geometric localization, we propose a regression-based decoder, which predicts the precise floating-point coordinates directly, without any coordinate quantization error. In the experiments, PolyFormer outperforms the prior art by a clear margin, e.g., 5.40% and 4.52% absolute improvements on the challenging RefCOCO+ and RefCOCOg datasets. It also shows strong generalization ability when evaluated on the referring video segmentation task without fine-tuning, e.g., achieving competitive 61.5% J&F on the Ref-DAVIS17 dataset. 在这项工作中，不是直接预测像素级分割掩模，而是将参考图像分割问题表述为顺序多边形生成，并且预测的多边形可以稍后转换为分割掩模。这是通过新的序列到序列框架 Polygon Transformer (PolyFormer) 实现的，该框架将一系列图像块和文本查询标记作为输入，并自回归输出一系列多边形顶点。为了更准确的几何定位，我们提出了一种基于回归的解码器，它直接预测精确的浮点坐标，没有任何坐标量化误差。在实验中，PolyFormer 明显优于现有技术，例如，在具有挑战性的 RefCOCO+ 和 RefCOCOg 数据集上绝对提高了 5.40% 和 4.52%。在无需微调的参考视频分割任务上进行评估时，它还显示出强大的泛化能力，例如，在 Ref-DAVIS17 数据集上实现了具有竞争力的 61.5% J&F。 将指代图像分割任务转化为顺序多边形生成，提出PolyFormer框架实现文本引导的精确分割。 Jiang Liu,Hui Ding,Zhaowei Cai,Yuting Zhang,Ravi Kumar Satzoda,Vijay Mahadevan,R. Manmatha cs.CV

authors_text: Zahra Gharaee,Felix Järemo Lawin,Per-Erik Forssén
title: Self-supervised learning of object pose estimation using keypoint prediction
title_zh: 使用关键点预测进行物体姿态估计的自监督学习
comment: 21 pages, 9 figures, 2 tables
comments: 21 pages, 9 figures, 2 tables
abstract: This paper describes recent developments in object specific pose and shape prediction from single images. The main contribution is a new approach to camera pose prediction by self-supervised learning of keypoints corresponding to locations on a category specific deformable shape. We designed a network to generate a proxy ground-truth heatmap from a set of keypoints distributed all over the category-specific mean shape, where each is represented by a unique color on a labeled texture. The proxy ground-truth heatmap is used to train a deep keypoint prediction network, which can be used in online inference. The proposed approach to camera pose prediction show significant improvements when compared with state-of-the-art methods. Our approach to camera pose prediction is used to infer 3D objects from 2D image frames of video sequences online. To train the reconstruction model, it receives only a silhouette mask from a single frame of a video sequence in every training step and a category-specific mean object shape. We conducted experiments using three different datasets representing the bird category: the CUB [51] image dataset, YouTubeVos and the Davis video datasets. The network is trained on the CUB dataset and tested on all three datasets. The online experiments are demonstrated on YouTubeVos and Davis [56] video sequences using a network trained on the CUB training set.
summary: This paper describes recent developments in object specific pose and shape prediction from single images. The main contribution is a new approach to camera pose prediction by self-supervised learning of keypoints corresponding to locations on a category specific deformable shape. We designed a network to generate a proxy ground-truth heatmap from a set of keypoints distributed all over the category-specific mean shape, where each is represented by a unique color on a labeled texture. The proxy ground-truth heatmap is used to train a deep keypoint prediction network, which can be used in online inference. The proposed approach to camera pose prediction show significant improvements when compared with state-of-the-art methods. Our approach to camera pose prediction is used to infer 3D objects from 2D image frames of video sequences online. To train the reconstruction model, it receives only a silhouette mask from a single frame of a video sequence in every training step and a category-specific mean object shape. We conducted experiments using three different datasets representing the bird category: the CUB [51] image dataset, YouTubeVos and the Davis video datasets. The network is trained on the CUB dataset and tested on all three datasets. The online experiments are demonstrated on YouTubeVos and Davis [56] video sequences using a network trained on the CUB training set.
abstract_zh: 本文描述了单图像对象特定姿势和形状预测的最新进展。主要贡献是通过自监督学习与特定类别可变形形状上的位置相对应的关键点来进行相机姿态预测的新方法。我们设计了一个网络，用于从分布在特定类别平均形状上的一组关键点生成代理地面实况热图，其中每个关键点都由标记纹理上的唯一颜色表示。代理地面实况热图用于训练深度关键点预测网络，可用于在线推理。与最先进的方法相比，所提出的相机姿态预测方法显示出显着的改进。我们的相机姿态预测方法用于从在线视频序列的 2D 图像帧推断 3D 对象。为了训练重建模型，它在每个训练步骤中仅接收来自视频序列的单帧的轮廓掩模和特定于类别的平均对象形状。我们使用代表鸟类类别的三个不同数据集进行了实验：CUB [51] 图像数据集、YouTubeVos 和 Davis 视频数据集。该网络在 CUB 数据集上进行训练，并在所有三个数据集上进行测试。使用在 CUB 训练集上训练的网络在 YouTubeVos 和 Davis [56] 视频序列上演示了在线实验。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自监督学习的物体姿态估计方法，通过预测关键点来实现从单张图像中推断3D物体姿态，并在视频序列中进行在线推理。
AI.motivation: 传统姿态估计依赖大量标注数据，而本文旨在通过自监督学习减少对人工标注的依赖，提升在复杂变形物体上的姿态预测性能。
AI.method: 设计网络生成基于类别特定平均形状的代理热图作为伪标签，利用单帧视频的轮廓掩码和平均形状训练关键点预测网络，最终实现在线3D姿态估计。
AI.result: 在CUB、YouTubeVos和Davis数据集上的实验表明，该方法在姿态预测任务中显著优于现有最先进方法，且能有效处理视频序列的在线推理。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的自监督关键点学习框架为单目姿态估计提供了新思路，验证了在无监督条件下对变形物体姿态预测的有效性和泛化能力。

authors_text: Minghao Liu,Zeyu Cheng,Shen Sang,Jing Liu,James Davis
title: Tag-based annotation creates better avatars
title_zh: 基于标签的注释创建更好的头像
comment: 15 pages, 7 figures, 4 tables
comments: 15 pages, 7 figures, 4 tables
abstract: Avatar creation from human images allows users to customize their digital figures in different styles. Existing rendering systems like Bitmoji, MetaHuman, and Google Cartoonset provide expressive rendering systems that serve as excellent design tools for users. However, twenty-plus parameters, some including hundreds of options, must be tuned to achieve ideal results. Thus it is challenging for users to create the perfect avatar. A machine learning model could be trained to predict avatars from images, however the annotators who label pairwise training data have the same difficulty as users, causing high label noise. In addition, each new rendering system or version update requires thousands of new training pairs. In this paper, we propose a Tag-based annotation method for avatar creation. Compared to direct annotation of labels, the proposed method: produces higher annotator agreements, causes machine learning to generates more consistent predictions, and only requires a marginal cost to add new rendering systems.
summary: Avatar creation from human images allows users to customize their digital figures in different styles. Existing rendering systems like Bitmoji, MetaHuman, and Google Cartoonset provide expressive rendering systems that serve as excellent design tools for users. However, twenty-plus parameters, some including hundreds of options, must be tuned to achieve ideal results. Thus it is challenging for users to create the perfect avatar. A machine learning model could be trained to predict avatars from images, however the annotators who label pairwise training data have the same difficulty as users, causing high label noise. In addition, each new rendering system or version update requires thousands of new training pairs. In this paper, we propose a Tag-based annotation method for avatar creation. Compared to direct annotation of labels, the proposed method: produces higher annotator agreements, causes machine learning to generates more consistent predictions, and only requires a marginal cost to add new rendering systems.
abstract_zh: 从人类图像创建头像允许用户以不同的风格定制他们的数字人物。 Bitmoji、MetaHuman 和 Google Cartoonset 等现有渲染系统提供了富有表现力的渲染系统，可以作为用户出色的设计工具。然而，必须调整二十多个参数，其中一些包括数百个选项，才能达到理想的结果。因此，创建完美的头像对于用户来说是一个挑战。可以训练机器学习模型来从图像中预测化身，但是标记成对训练数据的注释者与用户具有相同的困难，导致高标签噪声。此外，每个新的渲染系统或版本更新都需要数千个新的训练对。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于标签的头像创建标注方法。与直接标注标签相比，所提出的方法：产生更高的标注器一致性，使机器学习生成更一致的预测，并且只需要边际成本来添加新的渲染系统。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于标签的标注方法，用于改进Avatar生成，解决现有方法中参数过多和标注噪声高的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有Avatar创建系统需调整大量参数且标注数据噪声高，导致用户难以生成理想Avatar，且新系统更新成本高昂。
AI.method: 采用标签标注替代直接标注，通过简化标注流程提升一致性，并降低新增渲染系统时的训练成本。
AI.result: 该方法显著提高标注者一致性，使机器学习模型生成更稳定的Avatar预测，且新增系统仅需边际成本。
AI.conclusion: 标签标注方法有效解决了Avatar生成中的参数复杂性和标注噪声问题，为高效创建数字形象提供了新途径。

authors_text: Jonathan Michaux,Qingyi Chen,Yongseok Kwon,Ram Vasudevan
title: Reachability-based Trajectory Design with Neural Implicit Safety Constraints
title_zh: 具有神经隐式安全约束的基于可达性的轨迹设计
abstract: Generating safe motion plans in real-time is a key requirement for deploying robot manipulators to assist humans in collaborative settings. In particular, robots must satisfy strict safety requirements to avoid self-damage or harming nearby humans. Satisfying these requirements is particularly challenging if the robot must also operate in real-time to adjust to changes in its environment.This paper addresses these challenges by proposing Reachability-based Signed Distance Functions (RDFs) as a neural implicit representation for robot safety. RDF, which can be constructed using supervised learning in a tractable fashion, accurately predicts the distance between the swept volume of a robot arm and an obstacle. RDF's inference and gradient computations are fast and scale linearly with the dimension of the system; these features enable its use within a novel real-time trajectory planning framework as a continuous-time collision-avoidance constraint. The planning method using RDF is compared to a variety of state-of-the-art techniques and is demonstrated to successfully solve challenging motion planning tasks for high-dimensional systems faster and more reliably than all tested methods.
summary: Generating safe motion plans in real-time is a key requirement for deploying robot manipulators to assist humans in collaborative settings. In particular, robots must satisfy strict safety requirements to avoid self-damage or harming nearby humans. Satisfying these requirements is particularly challenging if the robot must also operate in real-time to adjust to changes in its environment.This paper addresses these challenges by proposing Reachability-based Signed Distance Functions (RDFs) as a neural implicit representation for robot safety. RDF, which can be constructed using supervised learning in a tractable fashion, accurately predicts the distance between the swept volume of a robot arm and an obstacle. RDF's inference and gradient computations are fast and scale linearly with the dimension of the system; these features enable its use within a novel real-time trajectory planning framework as a continuous-time collision-avoidance constraint. The planning method using RDF is compared to a variety of state-of-the-art techniques and is demonstrated to successfully solve challenging motion planning tasks for high-dimensional systems faster and more reliably than all tested methods.
abstract_zh: 实时生成安全运动计划是部署机器人操纵器以协助人类进行协作环境的关键要求。特别是，机器人必须满足严格的安全要求，以避免自我伤害或伤害附近的人类。如果机器人还必须实时运行以适应环境的变化，那么满足这些要求就特别具有挑战性。本文通过提出基于可达性的有符号距离函数（RDF）作为机器人安全的神经隐式表示来解决这些挑战。 RDF 可以使用监督学习以易于处理的方式构建，它可以准确预测机器人手臂扫过的体积与障碍物之间的距离。 RDF 的推理和梯度计算速度很快，并且与系统的维数成线性比例；这些功能使其能够在新颖的实时轨迹规划框架中用作连续时间防撞约束。使用 RDF 的规划方法与各种最先进的技术进行了比较，并被证明能够比所有测试方法更快、更可靠地成功解决高维系统的挑战性运动规划任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于可达性的轨迹设计方法，利用神经隐式安全约束实现实时安全运动规划。
AI.motivation: 协作机器人需要满足严格的安全要求以避免自身损坏或伤害人类，同时需在动态环境中实时调整轨迹，这对传统方法构成挑战。
AI.method: 提出可达性基于的符号距离函数（RDF），通过监督学习构建神经隐式表示，快速预测机械臂 swept volume 与障碍物的距离，并将其集成到实时轨迹规划框架中作为连续时间避障约束。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在高维系统中比现有技术更快更可靠地解决复杂运动规划任务。
AI.conclusion: RDF 提供了一种高效且可靠的实时安全运动规划解决方案，适用于高维动态环境中的机器人协作场景。

authors_text: Zuxin Liu,Zijian Guo,Yihang Yao,Zhepeng Cen,Wenhao Yu,Tingnan Zhang,Ding Zhao
title: Constrained Decision Transformer for Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 用于离线安全强化学习的约束决策转换器
comment: Published at ICML 2023
comments: Published at ICML 2023
abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) trains a constraint satisfaction policy by interacting with the environment. We aim to tackle a more challenging problem: learning a safe policy from an offline dataset. We study the offline safe RL problem from a novel multi-objective optimization perspective and propose the $ε$-reducible concept to characterize problem difficulties. The inherent trade-offs between safety and task performance inspire us to propose the constrained decision transformer (CDT) approach, which can dynamically adjust the trade-offs during deployment. Extensive experiments show the advantages of the proposed method in learning an adaptive, safe, robust, and high-reward policy. CDT outperforms its variants and strong offline safe RL baselines by a large margin with the same hyperparameters across all tasks, while keeping the zero-shot adaptation capability to different constraint thresholds, making our approach more suitable for real-world RL under constraints. The code is available at https://github.com/liuzuxin/OSRL.
summary: Safe reinforcement learning (RL) trains a constraint satisfaction policy by interacting with the environment. We aim to tackle a more challenging problem: learning a safe policy from an offline dataset. We study the offline safe RL problem from a novel multi-objective optimization perspective and propose the $ε$-reducible concept to characterize problem difficulties. The inherent trade-offs between safety and task performance inspire us to propose the constrained decision transformer (CDT) approach, which can dynamically adjust the trade-offs during deployment. Extensive experiments show the advantages of the proposed method in learning an adaptive, safe, robust, and high-reward policy. CDT outperforms its variants and strong offline safe RL baselines by a large margin with the same hyperparameters across all tasks, while keeping the zero-shot adaptation capability to different constraint thresholds, making our approach more suitable for real-world RL under constraints. The code is available at https://github.com/liuzuxin/OSRL.
abstract_zh: 安全强化学习（RL）通过与环境交互来训练约束满足策略。我们的目标是解决一个更具挑战性的问题：从离线数据集中学习安全策略。我们从新颖的多目标优化角度研究离线安全强化学习问题，并提出 $ε$-可约化概念来表征问题难度。安全性和任务性能之间固有的权衡激发我们提出约束决策转换器（CDT）方法，该方法可以在部署过程中动态调整权衡。大量的实验表明了该方法在学习自适应、安全、鲁棒和高回报策略方面的优势。 CDT 在所有任务中具有相同的超参数，大大优于其变体和强大的离线安全 RL 基线，同时保持对不同约束阈值的零样本适应能力，使我们的方法更适合约束下的现实世界 RL。代码可在 https://github.com/liuzuxin/OSRL 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于约束决策转换器（CDT）的离线安全强化学习方法，旨在从离线数据集中学习安全、高效且适应性强的策略。
AI.motivation: 传统安全强化学习依赖在线交互，而离线数据集学习面临安全约束与任务性能的权衡难题，现有方法难以兼顾安全性与适应性。
AI.method: 提出ε-可约概念表征问题难度，设计约束决策转换器（CDT）动态调整安全与性能的权衡，并通过多目标优化框架实现策略学习。
AI.result: 实验表明CDT在多个任务中显著优于现有离线安全RL方法，保持零样本适应能力，能灵活应对不同约束阈值。
AI.conclusion: CDT通过动态权衡机制和离线学习能力，为实际约束场景下的安全强化学习提供了高效且鲁棒的解决方案。

authors_text: J. Swaroop Guntupalli,Rajkumar Vasudeva Raju,Shrinu Kushagra,Carter Wendelken,Danny Sawyer,Ishan Deshpande,Guangyao Zhou,Miguel Lázaro-Gredilla,Dileep George
title: Graph schemas as abstractions for transfer learning, inference, and planning
title_zh: 图模式作为迁移学习、推理和规划的抽象
comment: 14 pages, 4 figures in main paper, 13 pages and 8 figures in appendix
comments: 14 pages, 4 figures in main paper, 13 pages and 8 figures in appendix
abstract: Transferring latent structure from one environment or problem to another is a mechanism by which humans and animals generalize with very little data. Inspired by cognitive and neurobiological insights, we propose graph schemas as a mechanism of abstraction for transfer learning. Graph schemas start with latent graph learning where perceptually aliased observations are disambiguated in the latent space using contextual information. Latent graph learning is also emerging as a new computational model of the hippocampus to explain map learning and transitive inference. Our insight is that a latent graph can be treated as a flexible template -- a schema -- that models concepts and behaviors, with slots that bind groups of latent nodes to the specific observations or groundings. By treating learned latent graphs (schemas) as prior knowledge, new environments can be quickly learned as compositions of schemas and their newly learned bindings. We evaluate graph schemas on two previously published challenging tasks: the memory & planning game and one-shot StreetLearn, which are designed to test rapid task solving in novel environments. Graph schemas can be learned in far fewer episodes than previous baselines, and can model and plan in a few steps in novel variations of these tasks. We also demonstrate learning, matching, and reusing graph schemas in more challenging 2D and 3D environments with extensive perceptual aliasing and size variations, and show how different schemas can be composed to model larger and more complex environments. To summarize, our main contribution is a unified system, inspired and grounded in cognitive science, that facilitates rapid transfer learning of new environments using schemas via map-induction and composition that handles perceptual aliasing.
summary: Transferring latent structure from one environment or problem to another is a mechanism by which humans and animals generalize with very little data. Inspired by cognitive and neurobiological insights, we propose graph schemas as a mechanism of abstraction for transfer learning. Graph schemas start with latent graph learning where perceptually aliased observations are disambiguated in the latent space using contextual information. Latent graph learning is also emerging as a new computational model of the hippocampus to explain map learning and transitive inference. Our insight is that a latent graph can be treated as a flexible template -- a schema -- that models concepts and behaviors, with slots that bind groups of latent nodes to the specific observations or groundings. By treating learned latent graphs (schemas) as prior knowledge, new environments can be quickly learned as compositions of schemas and their newly learned bindings. We evaluate graph schemas on two previously published challenging tasks: the memory & planning game and one-shot StreetLearn, which are designed to test rapid task solving in novel environments. Graph schemas can be learned in far fewer episodes than previous baselines, and can model and plan in a few steps in novel variations of these tasks. We also demonstrate learning, matching, and reusing graph schemas in more challenging 2D and 3D environments with extensive perceptual aliasing and size variations, and show how different schemas can be composed to model larger and more complex environments. To summarize, our main contribution is a unified system, inspired and grounded in cognitive science, that facilitates rapid transfer learning of new environments using schemas via map-induction and composition that handles perceptual aliasing.
abstract_zh: 将潜在结构从一个环境或问题转移到另一个环境或问题是人类和动物用很少的数据进行概括的机制。受认知和神经生物学见解的启发，我们提出图模式作为迁移学习的抽象机制。图模式从潜在图学习开始，其中使用上下文信息在潜在空间中消除感知别名观察的歧义。潜在图学习也正在作为一种新的海马计算模型出现，用于解释图学习和传递推理。我们的见解是，潜在图可以被视为一个灵活的模板——一种模式——对概念和行为进行建模，并带有将潜在节点组绑定到特定观察或基础的插槽。通过将学习到的潜在图（模式）视为先验知识，可以将新环境快速学习为模式及其新学习的绑定的组合。我们评估了之前发布的两项具有挑战性的任务的图模式：记忆和规划游戏和一次性 StreetLearn，它们旨在测试新环境中的快速任务解决。与以前的基线相比，图形模式可以用更少的片段来学习，并且可以在这些任务的新颖变体中通过几个步骤进行建模和规划。我们还演示了在更具挑战性的 2D 和 3D 环境中学习、匹配和重用图形模式，这些环境具有广泛的感知锯齿和大小变化，并展示了如何组合不同的模式来建模更大、更复杂的环境。总而言之，我们的主要贡献是一个统一的系统，其灵感来源于认知科学，并以认知科学为基础，通过映射归纳和组合处理感知混叠的模式，促进新环境的快速迁移学习。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于图模式的抽象机制，用于实现跨环境的迁移学习、推理和规划，通过在潜在空间中学习图结构并将其作为先验知识进行重用。
AI.motivation: 人类和动物能够通过少量数据泛化，受认知和神经生物学启发，需构建一种能处理感知混淆并快速适应新环境的抽象机制。
AI.method: 通过潜在图学习区分感知混淆的观测，利用上下文信息构建图模式（schema），将其作为可复用的模板，通过绑定新观测实现环境快速学习与组合。
AI.result: 在记忆与规划游戏和StreetLearn任务中，图模式方法比基线更少训练轮次即可完成任务，并能处理复杂2D/3D环境中的感知混淆与规模变化。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出了一种基于认知科学的统一系统，通过图模式的归纳与组合实现跨环境的快速迁移学习，有效解决了感知混淆问题。

search_text: Graph schemas as abstractions for transfer learning, inference, and planning 图模式作为迁移学习、推理和规划的抽象 Transferring latent structure from one environment or problem to another is a mechanism by which humans and animals generalize with very little data. Inspired by cognitive and neurobiological insights, we propose graph schemas as a mechanism of abstraction for transfer learning. Graph schemas start with latent graph learning where perceptually aliased observations are disambiguated in the latent space using contextual information. Latent graph learning is also emerging as a new computational model of the hippocampus to explain map learning and transitive inference. Our insight is that a latent graph can be treated as a flexible template -- a schema -- that models concepts and behaviors, with slots that bind groups of latent nodes to the specific observations or groundings. By treating learned latent graphs (schemas) as prior knowledge, new environments can be quickly learned as compositions of schemas and their newly learned bindings. We evaluate graph schemas on two previously published challenging tasks: the memory & planning game and one-shot StreetLearn, which are designed to test rapid task solving in novel environments. Graph schemas can be learned in far fewer episodes than previous baselines, and can model and plan in a few steps in novel variations of these tasks. We also demonstrate learning, matching, and reusing graph schemas in more challenging 2D and 3D environments with extensive perceptual aliasing and size variations, and show how different schemas can be composed to model larger and more complex environments. To summarize, our main contribution is a unified system, inspired and grounded in cognitive science, that facilitates rapid transfer learning of new environments using schemas via map-induction and composition that handles perceptual aliasing. 将潜在结构从一个环境或问题转移到另一个环境或问题是人类和动物用很少的数据进行概括的机制。受认知和神经生物学见解的启发，我们提出图模式作为迁移学习的抽象机制。图模式从潜在图学习开始，其中使用上下文信息在潜在空间中消除感知别名观察的歧义。潜在图学习也正在作为一种新的海马计算模型出现，用于解释图学习和传递推理。我们的见解是，潜在图可以被视为一个灵活的模板——一种模式——对概念和行为进行建模，并带有将潜在节点组绑定到特定观察或基础的插槽。通过将学习到的潜在图（模式）视为先验知识，可以将新环境快速学习为模式及其新学习的绑定的组合。我们评估了之前发布的两项具有挑战性的任务的图模式：记忆和规划游戏和一次性 StreetLearn，它们旨在测试新环境中的快速任务解决。与以前的基线相比，图形模式可以用更少的片段来学习，并且可以在这些任务的新颖变体中通过几个步骤进行建模和规划。我们还演示了在更具挑战性的 2D 和 3D 环境中学习、匹配和重用图形模式，这些环境具有广泛的感知锯齿和大小变化，并展示了如何组合不同的模式来建模更大、更复杂的环境。总而言之，我们的主要贡献是一个统一的系统，其灵感来源于认知科学，并以认知科学为基础，通过映射归纳和组合处理感知混叠的模式，促进新环境的快速迁移学习。 本文提出了一种基于图模式的抽象机制，用于实现跨环境的迁移学习、推理和规划，通过在潜在空间中学习图结构并将其作为先验知识进行重用。 J. authors_text: Tony T. Wang,Igor Zablotchi,Nir Shavit,Jonathan S. Rosenfeld
title: Cliff-Learning
title_zh: 悬崖学习
comment: 16 pages; v2 updates: improved layout, added acknowledgements
comments: 16 pages; v2 updates: improved layout, added acknowledgements
abstract: We study the data-scaling of transfer learning from foundation models in the low-downstream-data regime. We observe an intriguing phenomenon which we call cliff-learning. Cliff-learning refers to regions of data-scaling laws where performance improves at a faster than power law rate (i.e. regions of concavity on a log-log scaling plot). We conduct an in-depth investigation of foundation-model cliff-learning and study toy models of the phenomenon. We observe that the degree of cliff-learning reflects the degree of compatibility between the priors of a learning algorithm and the task being learned.
summary: We study the data-scaling of transfer learning from foundation models in the low-downstream-data regime. We observe an intriguing phenomenon which we call cliff-learning. Cliff-learning refers to regions of data-scaling laws where performance improves at a faster than power law rate (i.e. regions of concavity on a log-log scaling plot). We conduct an in-depth investigation of foundation-model cliff-learning and study toy models of the phenomenon. We observe that the degree of cliff-learning reflects the degree of compatibility between the priors of a learning algorithm and the task being learned.
abstract_zh: 我们研究低下游数据体系中基础模型的迁移学习的数据扩展。我们观察到一种有趣的现象，我们称之为悬崖学习。悬崖学习是指数据缩放定律的区域，其中性能以比幂律速率更快的速度提高（即对数缩放图上的凹区域）。我们对基础模型悬崖学习进行了深入调查，并研究了这种现象的玩具模型。我们观察到，悬崖学习的程度反映了学习算法的先验与正在学习的任务之间的兼容性程度。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在下游数据量较少时，基础模型迁移学习的数据扩展性，并提出了一种称为cliff-learning的现象。
AI.motivation: 传统模型在低数据量场景下表现受限，而基础模型可能通过迁移学习提升性能，但其数据扩展规律尚不明确。
AI.method: 通过分析数据扩展定律，结合玩具模型实验，研究cliff-learning现象及其与算法先验和任务兼容性的关系。
AI.result: 发现cliff-learning表现为性能提升快于幂律速率的区域，且其程度与算法先验和任务的兼容性密切相关。
AI.conclusion: cliff-learning现象揭示了基础模型迁移学习中先验与任务匹配的重要性，为优化模型设计提供新视角。

authors_text: Tongshuang Wu,Hua Shen,Daniel S. Weld,Jeffrey Heer,Marco Tulio Ribeiro
title: ScatterShot: Interactive In-context Example Curation for Text Transformation
title_zh: ScatterShot：用于文本转换的交互式上下文示例管理
comment: IUI 2023: 28th International Conference on Intelligent User Interfaces
comments: IUI 2023: 28th International Conference on Intelligent User Interfaces
abstract: The in-context learning capabilities of LLMs like GPT-3 allow annotators to customize an LLM to their specific tasks with a small number of examples. However, users tend to include only the most obvious patterns when crafting examples, resulting in underspecified in-context functions that fall short on unseen cases. Further, it is hard to know when "enough" examples have been included even for known patterns. In this work, we present ScatterShot, an interactive system for building high-quality demonstration sets for in-context learning. ScatterShot iteratively slices unlabeled data into task-specific patterns, samples informative inputs from underexplored or not-yet-saturated slices in an active learning manner, and helps users label more efficiently with the help of an LLM and the current example set. In simulation studies on two text perturbation scenarios, ScatterShot sampling improves the resulting few-shot functions by 4-5 percentage points over random sampling, with less variance as more examples are added. In a user study, ScatterShot greatly helps users in covering different patterns in the input space and labeling in-context examples more efficiently, resulting in better in-context learning and less user effort.
summary: The in-context learning capabilities of LLMs like GPT-3 allow annotators to customize an LLM to their specific tasks with a small number of examples. However, users tend to include only the most obvious patterns when crafting examples, resulting in underspecified in-context functions that fall short on unseen cases. Further, it is hard to know when "enough" examples have been included even for known patterns. In this work, we present ScatterShot, an interactive system for building high-quality demonstration sets for in-context learning. ScatterShot iteratively slices unlabeled data into task-specific patterns, samples informative inputs from underexplored or not-yet-saturated slices in an active learning manner, and helps users label more efficiently with the help of an LLM and the current example set. In simulation studies on two text perturbation scenarios, ScatterShot sampling improves the resulting few-shot functions by 4-5 percentage points over random sampling, with less variance as more examples are added. In a user study, ScatterShot greatly helps users in covering different patterns in the input space and labeling in-context examples more efficiently, resulting in better in-context learning and less user effort.
abstract_zh: GPT-3 等 LLM 的上下文学习功能允许注释者通过少量示例根据其特定任务定制 LLM。然而，用户在制作示例时往往只包含最明显的模式，从而导致未指定的上下文函数无法处理未见过的情况。此外，即使对于已知模式，也很难知道何时包含“足够”的示例。在这项工作中，我们展示了 ScatterShot，这是一个交互式系统，用于构建用于上下文学习的高质量演示集。 ScatterShot 迭代地将未标记的数据切片为特定于任务的模式，以主动学习的方式从未充分探索或尚未饱和的切片中采样信息输入，并在 LLM 和当前示例集的帮助下帮助用户更有效地进行标记。在对两种文本扰动场景的模拟研究中，ScatterShot 采样比随机采样将生成的少样本函数提高了 4-5 个百分点，并且随着添加更多示例，方差更小。在用户研究中，ScatterShot 极大地帮助用户覆盖输入空间中的不同模式并更有效地标记上下文示例，从而实现更好的上下文学习并减少用户工作量。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ScatterShot系统，通过交互式示例筛选提升基于上下文的文本转换任务效果。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在构建上下文示例时易忽略隐含模式，且难以判断示例数量是否充足，导致模型泛化能力不足。
AI.method: 采用主动学习策略，通过迭代划分数据模式、采样未探索区域样本，并结合LLM辅助标注，提升示例集质量。
AI.result: 模拟实验显示示例选择准确率提升4-5%，用户研究证实系统能有效覆盖更多输入模式并降低标注工作量。
AI.conclusion: ScatterShot通过智能示例筛选机制显著提升上下文学习效率，为少样本文本转换任务提供有效解决方案。

motivation: 现有方法在构建上下文示例时易忽略隐含模式，且难以判断示例数量是否充足，导致模型泛化能力不足。
method: 采用主动学习策略，通过迭代划分数据模式、采样未探索区域样本，并结合LLM辅助标注，提升示例集质量。
result: 模拟实验显示示例选择准确率提升4-5%，用户研究证实系统能有效覆盖更多输入模式并降低标注工作量。
conclusion: ScatterShot通过智能示例筛选机制显著提升上下文学习效率，为少样本文本转换任务提供有效解决方案。
search_text: ScatterShot: Interactive In-context Example Curation for Text Transformation ScatterShot：用于文本转换的交互式上下文示例管理 The in-context learning capabilities of LLMs like GPT-3 allow annotators to customize an LLM to their specific tasks with a small number of examples. However, users tend to include only the most obvious patterns when crafting examples, resulting in underspecified in-context functions that fall short on unseen cases. Further, it is hard to know when "enough" examples have been included even for known patterns. In this work, we present ScatterShot, an interactive system for building high-quality demonstration sets for in-context learning. ScatterShot iteratively slices unlabeled data into task-specific patterns, samples informative inputs from underexplored or not-yet-saturated slices in an active learning manner, and helps users label more efficiently with the help of an LLM and the current example set. In simulation studies on two text perturbation scenarios, ScatterShot sampling improves the resulting few-shot functions by 4-5 percentage points over random sampling, with less variance as more examples are added. In a user study, ScatterShot greatly helps users in covering different patterns in the input space and labeling in-context examples more efficiently, resulting in better in-context learning and less user effort. GPT-3 等 LLM 的上下文学习功能允许注释者通过少量示例根据其特定任务定制 LLM。然而，用户在制作示例时往往只包含最明显的模式，从而导致未指定的上下文函数无法处理未见过的情况。此外，即使对于已知模式，也很难知道何时包含“足够”的示例。在这项工作中，我们展示了 ScatterShot，这是一个交互式系统，用于构建用于上下文学习的高质量演示集。 ScatterShot 迭代地将未标记的数据切片为特定于任务的模式，以主动学习的方式从未充分探索或尚未饱和的切片中采样信息输入，并在 LLM 和当前示例集的帮助下帮助用户更有效地进行标记。在对两种文本扰动场景的模拟研究中，ScatterShot 采样比随机采样将生成的少样本函数提高了 4-5 个百分点，并且随着添加更多示例，方差更小。在用户研究中，ScatterShot 极大地帮助用户覆盖输入空间中的不同模式并更有效地标记上下文示例，从而实现更好的上下文学习并减少用户工作量。 本文提出ScatterShot系统，通过交互式示例筛选提升基于上下文的文本转换任务效果。 Tongshuang Wu,Hua Shen,Daniel S. Weld,Jeffrey Heer,Marco Tulio Ribeiro cs.HC,cs.CL

authors_text: Ke Wang,Zhaoyang Jacopo Hu,Peter Tisnikar,Oskar Helander,Digby Chappell,Petar Kormushev
title: When and Where to Step: Terrain-Aware Real-Time Footstep Location and Timing Optimization for Bipedal Robots
title_zh: 何时何地迈步：双足机器人的地形感知实时足迹位置和时序优化
comment: 32 pages, 15 figures. Submitted to Robotics and Autonomous Systems
comments: 32 pages, 15 figures. Submitted to Robotics and Autonomous Systems
abstract: Online footstep planning is essential for bipedal walking robots, allowing them to walk in the presence of disturbances and sensory noise. Most of the literature on the topic has focused on optimizing the footstep placement while keeping the step timing constant. In this work, we introduce a footstep planner capable of optimizing footstep placement and step time online. The proposed planner, consisting of an Interior Point Optimizer (IPOPT) and an optimizer based on Augmented Lagrangian (AL) method with analytical gradient descent, solves the full dynamics of the Linear Inverted Pendulum (LIP) model in real time to optimize for footstep location as well as step timing at the rate of 200~Hz. We show that such asynchronous real-time optimization with the AL method (ARTO-AL) provides the required robustness and speed for successful online footstep planning. Furthermore, ARTO-AL can be extended to plan footsteps in 3D, allowing terrain-aware footstep planning on uneven terrains. Compared to an algorithm with no footstep time adaptation, our proposed ARTO-AL demonstrates increased stability in simulated walking experiments as it can resist pushes on flat ground and on a $10^{\circ}$ ramp up to 120 N and 100 N respectively. For the video, see https://youtu.be/ABdnvPqCUu4. For code, see https://github.com/WangKeAlchemist/ARTO-AL/tree/master.
summary: Online footstep planning is essential for bipedal walking robots, allowing them to walk in the presence of disturbances and sensory noise. Most of the literature on the topic has focused on optimizing the footstep placement while keeping the step timing constant. In this work, we introduce a footstep planner capable of optimizing footstep placement and step time online. The proposed planner, consisting of an Interior Point Optimizer (IPOPT) and an optimizer based on Augmented Lagrangian (AL) method with analytical gradient descent, solves the full dynamics of the Linear Inverted Pendulum (LIP) model in real time to optimize for footstep location as well as step timing at the rate of 200~Hz. We show that such asynchronous real-time optimization with the AL method (ARTO-AL) provides the required robustness and speed for successful online footstep planning. Furthermore, ARTO-AL can be extended to plan footsteps in 3D, allowing terrain-aware footstep planning on uneven terrains. Compared to an algorithm with no footstep time adaptation, our proposed ARTO-AL demonstrates increased stability in simulated walking experiments as it can resist pushes on flat ground and on a $10^{\circ}$ ramp up to 120 N and 100 N respectively. For the video, see https://youtu.be/ABdnvPqCUu4. For code, see https://github.com/WangKeAlchemist/ARTO-AL/tree/master.
abstract_zh: 在线足迹规划对于双足行走机器人至关重要，使它们能够在存在干扰和感官噪音的情况下行走。大多数关于该主题的文献都集中在优化足迹位置，同时保持脚步时间恒定。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种足迹规划器，能够在线优化足迹位置和脚步时间。所提出的规划器由内点优化器 (IPOPT) 和基于解析梯度下降的增强拉格朗日 (AL) 方法的优化器组成，实时求解线性倒立摆 (LIP) 模型的完整动力学，以优化脚步位置以及 200Hz 速率下的脚步计时。我们证明，这种使用 AL 方法 (ARTO-AL) 的异步实时优化为成功的在线足迹规划提供了所需的稳健性和速度。此外，ARTO-AL 可以扩展为以 3D 方式规划足迹，从而可以在不平坦的地形上进行地形感知的足迹规划。与没有脚步时间自适应的算法相比，我们提出的 ARTO-AL 在模拟行走实验中表现出更高的稳定性，因为它可以抵抗平地上的推力和分别高达 120 N 和 100 N 的 10^{\circ}$ 斜坡上的推力。有关视频，请参阅 https://youtu.be/ABdnvPqCUu4。代码请参见https://github.com/WangKeAlchemist/ARTO-AL/tree/master。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种实时优化双足机器人足部位置和步态时间的规划方法，以适应复杂地形。
AI.motivation: 现有研究多关注足部位置优化而忽略步态时间调整，导致在动态环境和不平地形中稳定性不足。
AI.method: 结合内点优化器（IPOPT）和增广拉格朗日（AL）方法，实时求解线性倒立摆（LIP）模型动力学，实现200Hz的异步优化。
AI.result: 在模拟实验中，该方法在平面和10°斜坡上分别抵抗120N和100N推力，稳定性优于无时间自适应算法。
AI.conclusion: ARTO-AL方法通过联合优化足部位置与步态时间，显著提升了双足机器人在复杂地形中的实时行走稳定性。

authors_text: Levi Cai,Nathan E. McGuire,Roger Hanlon,T. Aran Mooney,Yogesh Girdhar
title: Semi-Supervised Visual Tracking of Marine Animals using Autonomous Underwater Vehicles
title_zh: 使用自主水下航行器对海洋动物进行半监督视觉跟踪
comment: To appear in IJCV SI: Animal Tracking
comments: To appear in IJCV SI: Animal Tracking
abstract: In-situ visual observations of marine organisms is crucial to developing behavioural understandings and their relations to their surrounding ecosystem. Typically, these observations are collected via divers, tags, and remotely-operated or human-piloted vehicles. Recently, however, autonomous underwater vehicles equipped with cameras and embedded computers with GPU capabilities are being developed for a variety of applications, and in particular, can be used to supplement these existing data collection mechanisms where human operation or tags are more difficult. Existing approaches have focused on using fully-supervised tracking methods, but labelled data for many underwater species are severely lacking. Semi-supervised trackers may offer alternative tracking solutions because they require less data than fully-supervised counterparts. However, because there are not existing realistic underwater tracking datasets, the performance of semi-supervised tracking algorithms in the marine domain is not well understood. To better evaluate their performance and utility, in this paper we provide (1) a novel dataset specific to marine animals located at http://warp.whoi.edu/vmat/, (2) an evaluation of state-of-the-art semi-supervised algorithms in the context of underwater animal tracking, and (3) an evaluation of real-world performance through demonstrations using a semi-supervised algorithm on-board an autonomous underwater vehicle to track marine animals in the wild.
summary: In-situ visual observations of marine organisms is crucial to developing behavioural understandings and their relations to their surrounding ecosystem. Typically, these observations are collected via divers, tags, and remotely-operated or human-piloted vehicles. Recently, however, autonomous underwater vehicles equipped with cameras and embedded computers with GPU capabilities are being developed for a variety of applications, and in particular, can be used to supplement these existing data collection mechanisms where human operation or tags are more difficult. Existing approaches have focused on using fully-supervised tracking methods, but labelled data for many underwater species are severely lacking. Semi-supervised trackers may offer alternative tracking solutions because they require less data than fully-supervised counterparts. However, because there are not existing realistic underwater tracking datasets, the performance of semi-supervised tracking algorithms in the marine domain is not well understood. To better evaluate their performance and utility, in this paper we provide (1) a novel dataset specific to marine animals located at http://warp.whoi.edu/vmat/, (2) an evaluation of state-of-the-art semi-supervised algorithms in the context of underwater animal tracking, and (3) an evaluation of real-world performance through demonstrations using a semi-supervised algorithm on-board an autonomous underwater vehicle to track marine animals in the wild.
abstract_zh: 对海洋生物的现场视觉观察对于发展行为理解及其与周围生态系统的关系至关重要。通常，这些观察结果是通过潜水员、标签和远程操作或人类驾驶的车辆收集的。然而，最近，配备摄像头和具有 GPU 功能的嵌入式计算机的自主水下航行器正在开发用于各种应用，特别是可用于补充这些现有的人类操作或标签较困难的数据收集机制。现有方法侧重于使用完全监督的跟踪方法，但严重缺乏许多水下物种的标记数据。半监督跟踪器可以提供替代的跟踪解决方案，因为它们比完全监督的跟踪器需要更少的数据。然而，由于不存在现实的水下跟踪数据集，半监督跟踪算法在海洋领域的性能尚不清楚。为了更好地评估它们的性能和实用性，在本文中，我们提供了（1）一个专门针对海洋动物的新颖数据集，位于http://warp.whoi.edu/vmat/，（2）在水下动物跟踪的背景下对最先进的半监督算法进行评估，以及（3）通过在自主水下航行器上使用半监督算法跟踪野外海洋动物的演示来评估现实世界的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自主水下航行器的半监督视觉追踪方法，用于追踪海洋动物，并构建了专用数据集进行评估。
AI.motivation: 现有全监督追踪方法因缺乏水下物种标注数据而受限，且传统数据采集方式（如潜水员、标签）存在局限性，需探索更高效的追踪方案。
AI.method: 开发了海洋动物专用数据集（http://warp.whoi.edu/vmat/），评估了先进半监督算法在水下环境中的性能，并通过自主水下航行器实测验证算法有效性。
AI.result: 成功构建数据集，验证了半监督算法在水下追踪中的可行性，并通过实际演示展示了算法在野外环境中的应用潜力。
AI.conclusion: 半监督方法可有效缓解水下标注数据不足问题，为海洋动物行为研究提供新工具，但需进一步优化算法适应复杂水下场景。

To better evaluate their performance and utility, in this paper we provide (1) a novel dataset specific to marine animals located at http://warp.whoi.edu/vmat/, (2) an evaluation of state-of-the-art semi-supervised algorithms in the context of underwater animal tracking, and (3) an evaluation of real-world performance through demonstrations using a semi-supervised algorithm on-board an autonomous underwater vehicle to track marine animals in the wild. 对海洋生物的现场视觉观察对于发展行为理解及其与周围生态系统的关系至关重要。通常，这些观察结果是通过潜水员、标签和远程操作或人类驾驶的车辆收集的。然而，最近，配备摄像头和具有 GPU 功能的嵌入式计算机的自主水下航行器正在开发用于各种应用，特别是可用于补充这些现有的人类操作或标签较困难的数据收集机制。现有方法侧重于使用完全监督的跟踪方法，但严重缺乏许多水下物种的标记数据。半监督跟踪器可以提供替代的跟踪解决方案，因为它们比完全监督的跟踪器需要更少的数据。然而，由于不存在现实的水下跟踪数据集，半监督跟踪算法在海洋领域的性能尚不清楚。为了更好地评估它们的性能和实用性，在本文中，我们提供了（1）一个专门针对海洋动物的新颖数据集，位于http://warp.whoi.edu/vmat/，（2）在水下动物跟踪的背景下对最先进的半监督算法进行评估，以及（3）通过在自主水下航行器上使用半监督算法跟踪野外海洋动物的演示来评估现实世界的性能。 本文提出了一种基于自主水下航行器的半监督视觉追踪方法，用于追踪海洋动物，并构建了专用数据集进行评估。 Levi Cai,Nathan E. McGuire,Roger Hanlon,T. Aran Mooney,Yogesh Girdhar cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.RO

authors_text: Milo Carroll,Zhaocheng Liu,Mohammadreza Kasaei,Zhibin Li
title: Agile and Versatile Robot Locomotion via Kernel-based Residual Learning
title_zh: 通过基于内核的残差学习实现敏捷且多功能的机器人运动
abstract: This work developed a kernel-based residual learning framework for quadrupedal robotic locomotion. Initially, a kernel neural network is trained with data collected from an MPC controller. Alongside a frozen kernel network, a residual controller network is trained via reinforcement learning to acquire generalized locomotion skills and resilience against external perturbations. With this proposed framework, a robust quadrupedal locomotion controller is learned with high sample efficiency and controllability, providing omnidirectional locomotion at continuous velocities. Its versatility and robustness are validated on unseen terrains that the expert MPC controller fails to traverse. Furthermore, the learned kernel can produce a range of functional locomotion behaviors and can generalize to unseen gaits.
summary: This work developed a kernel-based residual learning framework for quadrupedal robotic locomotion. Initially, a kernel neural network is trained with data collected from an MPC controller. Alongside a frozen kernel network, a residual controller network is trained via reinforcement learning to acquire generalized locomotion skills and resilience against external perturbations. With this proposed framework, a robust quadrupedal locomotion controller is learned with high sample efficiency and controllability, providing omnidirectional locomotion at continuous velocities. Its versatility and robustness are validated on unseen terrains that the expert MPC controller fails to traverse. Furthermore, the learned kernel can produce a range of functional locomotion behaviors and can generalize to unseen gaits.
abstract_zh: 这项工作开发了一个基于内核的四足机器人运动残差学习框架。最初，使用从 MPC 控制器收集的数据来训练内核神经网络。除了冻结的内核网络之外，还通过强化学习来训练残差控制器网络，以获得广义的运动技能和抵御外部扰动的能力。通过该框架，可以学习到具有高采样效率和可控性的鲁棒四足运动控制器，从而以连续速度提供全向运动。其多功能性和稳健性在专家 MPC 控制器无法穿越的未知地形上得到了验证。此外，学习到的内核可以产生一系列功能性运动行为，并可以推广到看不见的步态。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于核函数的残差学习框架，用于实现四足机器人的敏捷且多功能的运动控制。
AI.motivation: 传统模型预测控制（MPC）在复杂地形和外部扰动下表现不足，需开发更鲁棒且通用的运动控制器。
AI.method: 结合核神经网络与强化学习训练残差控制器，前者由MPC数据预训练，后者通过强化学习学习通用运动技能并增强抗干扰能力。
AI.result: 框架在未知地形中实现全向运动，样本效率高且可控性强，核网络可生成多样化步态并泛化到新场景。
AI.conclusion: 该框架验证了核残差学习在四足机器人运动控制中的有效性，显著提升了鲁棒性和适应性。

authors_text: Malintha Fernando,Ransalu Senanayake,Heeyoul Choi,Martin Swany
title: Graph Attention Multi-Agent Fleet Autonomy for Advanced Air Mobility
title_zh: 用于先进空中机动性的图注意力多智能体机队自治
comment: Accepted to Robotics: Science and Systems, 2023. 14 pages, 13 figures, 3 tables
comments: Accepted to Robotics: Science and Systems, 2023. 14 pages, 13 figures, 3 tables
abstract: Autonomous mobility is emerging as a new disruptive mode of urban transportation for moving cargo and passengers. However, designing scalable autonomous fleet coordination schemes to accommodate fast-growing mobility systems is challenging primarily due to the increasing heterogeneity of the fleets, time-varying demand patterns, service area expansions, and communication limitations. We introduce the concept of partially observable advanced air mobility games to coordinate a fleet of aerial vehicles by accounting for the heterogeneity of the interacting agents and the self-interested nature inherent to commercial mobility fleets. To model the complex interactions among the agents and the observation uncertainty in the mobility networks, we propose a novel heterogeneous graph attention encoder-decoder (HetGAT Enc-Dec) neural network-based stochastic policy. We train the policy by leveraging deep multi-agent reinforcement learning, allowing decentralized decision-making for the agents using their local observations. Through extensive experimentation, we show that the learned policy generalizes to various fleet compositions, demand patterns, and observation topologies. Further, fleets operating under the HetGAT Enc-Dec policy outperform other state-of-the-art graph neural network policies by achieving the highest fleet reward and fulfillment ratios in on-demand mobility networks.
summary: Autonomous mobility is emerging as a new disruptive mode of urban transportation for moving cargo and passengers. However, designing scalable autonomous fleet coordination schemes to accommodate fast-growing mobility systems is challenging primarily due to the increasing heterogeneity of the fleets, time-varying demand patterns, service area expansions, and communication limitations. We introduce the concept of partially observable advanced air mobility games to coordinate a fleet of aerial vehicles by accounting for the heterogeneity of the interacting agents and the self-interested nature inherent to commercial mobility fleets. To model the complex interactions among the agents and the observation uncertainty in the mobility networks, we propose a novel heterogeneous graph attention encoder-decoder (HetGAT Enc-Dec) neural network-based stochastic policy. We train the policy by leveraging deep multi-agent reinforcement learning, allowing decentralized decision-making for the agents using their local observations. Through extensive experimentation, we show that the learned policy generalizes to various fleet compositions, demand patterns, and observation topologies. Further, fleets operating under the HetGAT Enc-Dec policy outperform other state-of-the-art graph neural network policies by achieving the highest fleet reward and fulfillment ratios in on-demand mobility networks.
abstract_zh: 自动驾驶移动正在成为一种新的颠覆性城市交通运输货物和乘客的模式。然而，设计可扩展的自主车队协调方案以适应快速增长的移动系统具有挑战性，这主要是由于车队的异质性不断增加、随时间变化的需求模式、服务区域扩展和通信限制。我们引入了部分可观察的先进空中机动博弈的概念，通过考虑交互代理的异质性和商业机动机队固有的自利性质来协调飞行器机队。为了对智能体之间的复杂交互和移动网络中的观察不确定性进行建模，我们提出了一种新颖的基于异构图注意力编码器-解码器（HetGAT Enc-Dec）神经网络的随机策略。我们通过利用深度多智能体强化学习来训练策略，允许智能体使用本地观察结果进行去中心化决策。通过广泛的实验，我们表明学习的策略可以推广到各种机队组成、需求模式和观察拓扑。此外，在 HetGAT Enc-Dec 政策下运营的车队在按需移动网络中实现了最高的车队奖励和履行率，从而优于其他最先进的图神经网络策略。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于异质图注意力网络的多智能体自主协调框架，用于解决先进空中交通中的舰队调度问题。
AI.motivation: 现有自主舰队协调方案难以应对异质性、动态需求、服务区域扩展及通信限制等挑战，需开发更适应复杂环境的智能决策系统。
AI.method: 设计HetGAT Enc-Dec异质图注意力编码器-解码器网络，结合深度多智能体强化学习，实现基于局部观测的去中心化决策。
AI.result: 实验表明该策略在不同舰队配置、需求模式和观测拓扑中均表现优异，相比其他图神经网络方法取得更高舰队奖励和任务完成率。
AI.conclusion: HetGAT Enc-Dec框架有效解决了异质多智能体协同问题，为动态环境下的空中交通管理提供了可扩展的解决方案。

authors_text: Zheqi Lv,Wenqiao Zhang,Zhengyu Chen,Shengyu Zhang,Kun Kuang
title: Intelligent Model Update Strategy for Sequential Recommendation
title_zh: 顺序推荐的智能模型更新策略
comment: Published on WWW'24(Oral): Proceedings of the ACM on Web Conference 2024 (pp. 3117-3128)
comments: Published on WWW'24(Oral): Proceedings of the ACM on Web Conference 2024 (pp. 3117-3128)
abstract: Modern online platforms are increasingly employing recommendation systems to address information overload and improve user engagement. There is an evolving paradigm in this research field that recommendation network learning occurs both on the cloud and on edges with knowledge transfer in between (i.e., edge-cloud collaboration). Recent works push this field further by enabling edge-specific context-aware adaptivity, where model parameters are updated in real-time based on incoming on-edge data. However, we argue that frequent data exchanges between the cloud and edges often lead to inefficiency and waste of communication/computation resources, as considerable parameter updates might be redundant. To investigate this problem, we introduce Intelligent Edge-Cloud Parameter Request Model, abbreviated as IntellectReq. IntellectReq is designed to operate on edge, evaluating the cost-benefit landscape of parameter requests with minimal computation and communication overhead. We formulate this as a novel learning task, aimed at the detection of out-of-distribution data, thereby fine-tuning adaptive communication strategies. Further, we employ statistical mapping techniques to convert real-time user behavior into a normal distribution, thereby employing multi-sample outputs to quantify the model's uncertainty and thus its generalization capabilities. Rigorous empirical validation on four widely-adopted benchmarks evaluates our approach, evidencing a marked improvement in the efficiency and generalizability of edge-cloud collaborative and dynamic recommendation systems.
summary: Modern online platforms are increasingly employing recommendation systems to address information overload and improve user engagement. There is an evolving paradigm in this research field that recommendation network learning occurs both on the cloud and on edges with knowledge transfer in between (i.e., edge-cloud collaboration). However, we argue that frequent data exchanges between the cloud and edges often lead to inefficiency and waste of communication/computation resources, as considerable parameter updates might be redundant. To investigate this problem, we introduce Intelligent Edge-Cloud Parameter Request Model, abbreviated as IntellectReq. IntellectReq is designed to operate on edge, evaluating the cost-benefit landscape of parameter requests with minimal computation and communication overhead. We formulate this as a novel learning task, aimed at the detection of out-of-distribution data, thereby fine-tuning adaptive communication strategies. Further, we employ statistical mapping techniques to convert real-time user behavior into a normal distribution, thereby employing multi-sample outputs to quantify the model's uncertainty and thus its generalization capabilities. Rigorous empirical validation on four widely-adopted benchmarks evaluates our approach, evidencing a marked improvement in the efficiency and generalizability of edge-cloud collaborative and dynamic recommendation systems.
abstract_zh: 现代在线平台越来越多地采用推荐系统来解决信息过载问题并提高用户参与度。该研究领域有一个不断发展的范式，即推荐网络学习同时发生在云和边缘上，并在两者之间进行知识转移（即边缘云协作）。最近的工作通过实现特定于边缘的上下文感知自适应性进一步推动了这一领域，其中模型参数根据传入的边缘数据实时更新。然而，我们认为云和边缘之间频繁的数据交换通常会导致通信/计算资源的低效率和浪费，因为大量的参数更新可能是多余的。为了研究这个问题，我们引入了智能边缘-云参数请求模型，缩写为IntellectReq。 IntellectReq 设计为在边缘运行，以最小的计算和通信开销评估参数请求的成本效益情况。我们将其制定为一项新颖的学习任务，旨在检测分布外数据，从而微调自适应通信策略。此外，我们采用统计映射技术将实时用户行为转换为正态分布，从而采用多样本输出来量化模型的不确定性及其泛化能力。对四个广泛采用的基准进行严格的实证验证评估了我们的方法，证明边缘云协作和动态推荐系统的效率和通用性有了显着提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种智能边缘-云参数请求模型（IntellectReq），旨在优化边缘设备与云端协作的推荐系统中参数更新策略，减少冗余通信与计算资源浪费。
AI.motivation: 现有边缘-云协同推荐系统因频繁参数交互导致通信与计算资源浪费，且大量参数更新可能冗余，需更高效的动态适应策略。
AI.method: 设计IntellectReq模型，在边缘端通过低成本计算评估参数请求的收益与代价，结合分布外检测与统计映射技术，将实时用户行为转化为正态分布以量化模型不确定性，指导自适应通信策略。
AI.result: 在四个基准数据集上验证，证明该方法显著提升边缘-云协同推荐系统的效率与泛化能力，减少冗余更新带来的资源消耗。
AI.conclusion: IntellectReq通过智能参数请求机制有效平衡边缘与云端协作的资源开销，为动态推荐系统提供高效且通用的解决方案。

search_text: Intelligent Model Update Strategy for Sequential Recommendation 顺序推荐的智能模型更新策略 Modern online platforms are increasingly employing recommendation systems to address information overload and improve user engagement. There is an evolving paradigm in this research field that recommendation network learning occurs both on the cloud and on edges with knowledge transfer in between (i.e., edge-cloud collaboration). Recent works push this field further by enabling edge-specific context-aware adaptivity, where model parameters are updated in real-time based on incoming on-edge data. However, we argue that frequent data exchanges between the cloud and edges often lead to inefficiency and waste of communication/computation resources, as considerable parameter updates might be redundant. To investigate this problem, we introduce Intelligent Edge-Cloud Parameter Request Model, abbreviated as IntellectReq. IntellectReq is designed to operate on edge, evaluating the cost-benefit landscape of parameter requests with minimal computation and communication overhead. We formulate this as a novel learning task, aimed at the detection of out-of-distribution data, thereby fine-tuning adaptive communication strategies. Further, we employ statistical mapping techniques to convert real-time user behavior into a normal distribution, thereby employing multi-sample outputs to quantify the model's uncertainty and thus its generalization capabilities. Rigorous empirical validation on four widely-adopted benchmarks evaluates our approach, evidencing a marked improvement in the efficiency and generalizability of edge-cloud collaborative and dynamic recommendation systems. 现代在线平台越来越多地采用推荐系统来解决信息过载问题并提高用户参与度。该研究领域有一个不断发展的范式，即推荐网络学习同时发生在云和边缘上，并在两者之间进行知识转移（即边缘云协作）。最近的工作通过实现特定于边缘的上下文感知自适应性进一步推动了这一领域，其中模型参数根据传入的边缘数据实时更新。然而，我们认为云和边缘之间频繁的数据交换通常会导致通信/计算资源的低效率和浪费，因为大量的参数更新可能是多余的。为了研究这个问题，我们引入了智能边缘-云参数请求模型，缩写为IntellectReq。 IntellectReq 设计为在边缘运行，以最小的计算和通信开销评估参数请求的成本效益情况。我们将其制定为一项新颖的学习任务，旨在检测分布外数据，从而微调自适应通信策略。此外，我们采用统计映射技术将实时用户行为转换为正态分布，从而采用多样本输出来量化模型的不确定性及其泛化能力。对四个广泛采用的基准进行严格的实证验证评估了我们的方法，证明边缘云协作和动态推荐系统的效率和通用性有了显着提高。 本文提出一种智能边缘-云参数请求模型（IntellectReq），旨在优化边缘设备与云端协作的推荐系统中参数更新策略，减少冗余通信与计算资源浪费。 Zheqi Lv,Wenqiao Zhang,Zhengyu Chen,Shengyu Zhang,Kun Kuang cs.IR,cs.AI,cs.DC

authors_text: Ye Yue,Marc Baltes,Nidal Abujahar,Tao Sun,Charles D. Smith,Trevor Bihl,Jundong Liu
title: Hybrid Spiking Neural Network Fine-tuning for Hippocampus Segmentation
title_zh: 用于海马分割的混合尖峰神经网络微调
comment: Accepted to ISBI 2023 conference
comments: Accepted to ISBI 2023 conference
abstract: Over the past decade, artificial neural networks (ANNs) have made tremendous advances, in part due to the increased availability of annotated data. However, ANNs typically require significant power and memory consumptions to reach their full potential. Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have recently emerged as a low-power alternative to ANNs due to their sparsity nature. SNN, however, are not as easy to train as ANNs. In this work, we propose a hybrid SNN training scheme and apply it to segment human hippocampi from magnetic resonance images. Our approach takes ANN-SNN conversion as an initialization step and relies on spike-based backpropagation to fine-tune the network. Compared with the conversion and direct training solutions, our method has advantages in both segmentation accuracy and training efficiency. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our model in achieving the design goals.
summary: Over the past decade, artificial neural networks (ANNs) have made tremendous advances, in part due to the increased availability of annotated data. However, ANNs typically require significant power and memory consumptions to reach their full potential. Spiking neural networks (SNNs) have recently emerged as a low-power alternative to ANNs due to their sparsity nature. SNN, however, are not as easy to train as ANNs. In this work, we propose a hybrid SNN training scheme and apply it to segment human hippocampi from magnetic resonance images. Our approach takes ANN-SNN conversion as an initialization step and relies on spike-based backpropagation to fine-tune the network. Compared with the conversion and direct training solutions, our method has advantages in both segmentation accuracy and training efficiency. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our model in achieving the design goals.
abstract_zh: 在过去的十年中，人工神经网络 (ANN) 取得了巨大的进步，部分原因是注释数据的可用性不断提高。然而，人工神经网络通常需要大量的功耗和内存消耗才能充分发挥其潜力。由于其稀疏性，尖峰神经网络 (SNN) 最近已成为 ANN 的低功耗替代品。然而，SNN 不像 ANN 那样容易训练。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种混合 SNN 训练方案，并将其应用于从磁共振图像中分割人类海马体。我们的方法将 ANN-SNN 转换作为初始化步骤，并依靠基于尖峰的反向传播来微调网络。与转换和直接训练方案相比，我们的方法在分割精度和训练效率上都具有优势。实验证明了我们的模型在实现设计目标方面的有效性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种混合脉冲神经网络微调方法，用于从磁共振图像中分割人脑海马体。
AI.motivation: 传统人工神经网络(ANN)在分割任务中表现优异但能耗高，脉冲神经网络(SNN)虽节能但训练困难，需寻找兼顾效率与精度的解决方案。
AI.method: 将ANN转换为SNN作为初始化步骤，结合基于脉冲的反向传播算法进行微调，形成混合训练框架。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在分割精度和训练效率上优于直接训练和转换方案，有效达成设计目标。
AI.conclusion: 混合SNN微调方法成功平衡了海马体分割任务的精度需求与能效约束，为神经网络应用提供新思路。

authors_text: Jieming Bian,Cong Shen,Jie Xu
title: Federated Learning via Indirect Server-Client Communications
title_zh: 通过间接服务器-客户端通信进行联合学习
comment: 6 pages
comments: 6 pages
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a communication-efficient and privacy-preserving distributed machine learning framework that has gained a significant amount of research attention recently. Despite the different forms of FL algorithms (e.g., synchronous FL, asynchronous FL) and the underlying optimization methods, nearly all existing works implicitly assumed the existence of a communication infrastructure that facilitates the direct communication between the server and the clients for the model data exchange. This assumption, however, does not hold in many real-world applications that can benefit from distributed learning but lack a proper communication infrastructure (e.g., smart sensing in remote areas). In this paper, we propose a novel FL framework, named FedEx (short for FL via Model Express Delivery), that utilizes mobile transporters (e.g., Unmanned Aerial Vehicles) to establish indirect communication channels between the server and the clients. Two algorithms, called FedEx-Sync and FedEx-Async, are developed depending on whether the transporters adopt a synchronized or an asynchronized schedule. Even though the indirect communications introduce heterogeneous delays to clients for both the global model dissemination and the local model collection, we prove the convergence of both versions of FedEx. The convergence analysis subsequently sheds lights on how to assign clients to different transporters and design the routes among the clients. The performance of FedEx is evaluated through experiments in a simulated network on two public datasets.
summary: Federated Learning (FL) is a communication-efficient and privacy-preserving distributed machine learning framework that has gained a significant amount of research attention recently. Despite the different forms of FL algorithms (e.g., synchronous FL, asynchronous FL) and the underlying optimization methods, nearly all existing works implicitly assumed the existence of a communication infrastructure that facilitates the direct communication between the server and the clients for the model data exchange. This assumption, however, does not hold in many real-world applications that can benefit from distributed learning but lack a proper communication infrastructure (e.g., smart sensing in remote areas). In this paper, we propose a novel FL framework, named FedEx (short for FL via Model Express Delivery), that utilizes mobile transporters (e.g., Unmanned Aerial Vehicles) to establish indirect communication channels between the server and the clients. Two algorithms, called FedEx-Sync and FedEx-Async, are developed depending on whether the transporters adopt a synchronized or an asynchronized schedule. Even though the indirect communications introduce heterogeneous delays to clients for both the global model dissemination and the local model collection, we prove the convergence of both versions of FedEx. The convergence analysis subsequently sheds lights on how to assign clients to different transporters and design the routes among the clients. The performance of FedEx is evaluated through experiments in a simulated network on two public datasets.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习（FL）是一种高效通信且保护隐私的分布式机器学习框架，最近引起了广泛的研究关注。尽管 FL 算法有不同的形式（例如，同步 FL、异步 FL）和底层优化方法，但几乎所有现有工作都隐含地假设存在通信基础设施，以促进服务器和客户端之间的直接通信以进行模型数据交换。然而，这种假设在许多可以从分布式学习中受益但缺乏适当的通信基础设施（例如偏远地区的智能传感）的实际应用中并不成立。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的 FL 框架，名为 FedEx（FL via Model Express Delivery 的缩写），它利用移动运输工具（例如无人机）在服务器和客户端之间建立间接通信通道。两种算法（称为 FedEx-Sync 和 FedEx-Async）是根据运输商采用同步还是异步时间表而开发的。尽管间接通信在全球模型传播和本地模型收集方面给客户带来了异构延迟，但我们证明了两个版本的 FedEx 的收敛性。随后的收敛分析揭示了如何将客户端分配给不同的传输者并设计客户端之间的路线。联邦快递的性能是通过在两个公共数据集上的模拟网络中进行的实验来评估的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为FedEx的联邦学习框架，通过移动运输工具（如无人机）建立间接通信渠道，解决缺乏直接通信基础设施场景下的分布式学习问题。
AI.motivation: 传统联邦学习依赖直接的服务器-客户端通信，但在偏远地区等缺乏通信基础设施的场景中难以应用，亟需新的通信方案。
AI.method: 设计FedEx框架利用无人机等移动运输工具实现间接通信，开发同步（FedEx-Sync）与异步（FedEx-Async）两种算法，并通过收敛性分析指导客户端分配与运输路径设计。
AI.result: 在模拟网络环境中使用公共数据集验证FedEx有效性，证明其能在异构延迟下保持收敛性。

search_text: Federated Learning via Indirect Server-Client Communications 通过间接服务器-客户端通信进行联合学习 Federated Learning (FL) is a communication-efficient and privacy-preserving distributed machine learning framework that has gained a significant amount of research attention recently. Despite the different forms of FL algorithms (e.g., synchronous FL, asynchronous FL) and the underlying optimization methods, nearly all existing works implicitly assumed the existence of a communication infrastructure that facilitates the direct communication between the server and the clients for the model data exchange. This assumption, however, does not hold in many real-world applications that can benefit from distributed learning but lack a proper communication infrastructure (e.g., smart sensing in remote areas). In this paper, we propose a novel FL framework, named FedEx (short for FL via Model Express Delivery), that utilizes mobile transporters (e.g., Unmanned Aerial Vehicles) to establish indirect communication channels between the server and the clients. Two algorithms, called FedEx-Sync and FedEx-Async, are developed depending on whether the transporters adopt a synchronized or an asynchronized schedule. Even though the indirect communications introduce heterogeneous delays to clients for both the global model dissemination and the local model collection, we prove the convergence of both versions of FedEx. The convergence analysis subsequently sheds lights on how to assign clients to different transporters and design the routes among the clients. The performance of FedEx is evaluated through experiments in a simulated network on two public datasets. 联邦学习（FL）是一种高效通信且保护隐私的分布式机器学习框架，最近引起了广泛的研究关注。尽管 FL 算法有不同的形式（例如，同步 FL、异步 FL）和底层优化方法，但几乎所有现有工作都隐含地假设存在通信基础设施，以促进服务器和客户端之间的直接通信以进行模型数据交换。然而，这种假设在许多可以从分布式学习中受益但缺乏适当的通信基础设施（例如偏远地区的智能传感）的实际应用中并不成立。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的 FL 框架，名为 FedEx（FL via Model Express Delivery 的缩写），它利用移动运输工具（例如无人机）在服务器和客户端之间建立间接通信通道。两种算法（称为 FedEx-Sync 和 FedEx-Async）是根据运输商采用同步还是异步时间表而开发的。尽管间接通信在全球模型传播和本地模型收集方面给客户带来了异构延迟，但我们证明了两个版本的 FedEx 的收敛性。随后的收敛分析揭示了如何将客户端分配给不同的传输者并设计客户端之间的路线。联邦快递的性能是通过在两个公共数据集上的模拟网络中进行的实验来评估的。 本文提出一种名为FedEx的联邦学习框架，通过移动运输工具（如无人机）建立间接通信渠道，解决缺乏直接通信基础设施场景下的分布式学习问题。 Jieming Bian,Cong Shen,Jie Xu cs.DC,cs.AI

authors_text: Siddhesh Khandelwal,Anirudth Nambirajan,Behjat Siddiquie,Jayan Eledath,Leonid Sigal
title: Frustratingly Simple but Effective Zero-shot Detection and Segmentation: Analysis and a Strong Baseline
title_zh: 令人沮丧的简单但有效的零样本检测和分割：分析和强大的基线
comment: 17 Pages, 7 Figures
comments: 17 Pages, 7 Figures
abstract: Methods for object detection and segmentation often require abundant instance-level annotations for training, which are time-consuming and expensive to collect. To address this, the task of zero-shot object detection (or segmentation) aims at learning effective methods for identifying and localizing object instances for the categories that have no supervision available. Constructing architectures for these tasks requires choosing from a myriad of design options, ranging from the form of the class encoding used to transfer information from seen to unseen categories, to the nature of the function being optimized for learning. In this work, we extensively study these design choices, and carefully construct a simple yet extremely effective zero-shot recognition method. Through extensive experiments on the MSCOCO dataset on object detection and segmentation, we highlight that our proposed method outperforms existing, considerably more complex, architectures. Our findings and method, which we propose as a competitive future baseline, point towards the need to revisit some of the recent design trends in zero-shot detection / segmentation.
summary: Methods for object detection and segmentation often require abundant instance-level annotations for training, which are time-consuming and expensive to collect. To address this, the task of zero-shot object detection (or segmentation) aims at learning effective methods for identifying and localizing object instances for the categories that have no supervision available. Constructing architectures for these tasks requires choosing from a myriad of design options, ranging from the form of the class encoding used to transfer information from seen to unseen categories, to the nature of the function being optimized for learning. In this work, we extensively study these design choices, and carefully construct a simple yet extremely effective zero-shot recognition method. Through extensive experiments on the MSCOCO dataset on object detection and segmentation, we highlight that our proposed method outperforms existing, considerably more complex, architectures. Our findings and method, which we propose as a competitive future baseline, point towards the need to revisit some of the recent design trends in zero-shot detection / segmentation.
abstract_zh: 对象检测和分割方法通常需要大量实例级注释进行训练，收集这些注释既耗时又昂贵。为了解决这个问题，零样本对象检测（或分割）的任务旨在学习有效的方法来识别和定位没有可用监督的类别的对象实例。为这些任务构建架构需要从无数的设计选项中进行选择，从用于将信息从可见类别传输到不可见类别的类编码形式，到为学习而优化的函数的性质。在这项工作中，我们广泛研究了这些设计选择，并仔细构建了一种简单但极其有效的零样本识别方法。通过在 MSCOCO 数据集上进行对象检测和分割的大量实验，我们强调我们提出的方法优于现有的、复杂得多的架构。我们提出作为未来竞争基准的研究结果和方法表明需要重新审视零样本检测/分割的一些最新设计趋势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种简单但高效的零样本目标检测与分割方法，无需对未见类别进行标注。
AI.motivation: 传统目标检测和分割方法依赖大量实例标注，成本高昂且耗时，而零样本方法旨在无需监督的情况下识别未见类别。
AI.method: 通过分析类编码形式和优化函数设计，构建了一个简单但有效的零样本识别框架，并在MSCOCO数据集上验证。
AI.result: 该方法在零样本检测和分割任务中超越了现有复杂架构，性能显著提升。
AI.conclusion: 提出的简单方法可作为未来研究的强基线，提示需重新审视零样本检测/分割的近期设计趋势。

authors_text: Jifan Zhang,Shuai Shao,Saurabh Verma,Robert Nowak
title: Algorithm Selection for Deep Active Learning with Imbalanced Datasets
title_zh: 不平衡数据集深度主动学习的算法选择
abstract: Label efficiency has become an increasingly important objective in deep learning applications. Active learning aims to reduce the number of labeled examples needed to train deep networks, but the empirical performance of active learning algorithms can vary dramatically across datasets and applications. It is difficult to know in advance which active learning strategy will perform well or best in a given application. To address this, we propose the first adaptive algorithm selection strategy for deep active learning. For any unlabeled dataset, our (meta) algorithm TAILOR (Thompson ActIve Learning algORithm selection) iteratively and adaptively chooses among a set of candidate active learning algorithms. TAILOR uses novel reward functions aimed at gathering class-balanced examples. Extensive experiments in multi-class and multi-label applications demonstrate TAILOR's effectiveness in achieving accuracy comparable or better than that of the best of the candidate algorithms. Our implementation of TAILOR is open-sourced at https://github.com/jifanz/TAILOR.
summary: Label efficiency has become an increasingly important objective in deep learning applications. Active learning aims to reduce the number of labeled examples needed to train deep networks, but the empirical performance of active learning algorithms can vary dramatically across datasets and applications. It is difficult to know in advance which active learning strategy will perform well or best in a given application. To address this, we propose the first adaptive algorithm selection strategy for deep active learning. For any unlabeled dataset, our (meta) algorithm TAILOR (Thompson ActIve Learning algORithm selection) iteratively and adaptively chooses among a set of candidate active learning algorithms. TAILOR uses novel reward functions aimed at gathering class-balanced examples. Extensive experiments in multi-class and multi-label applications demonstrate TAILOR's effectiveness in achieving accuracy comparable or better than that of the best of the candidate algorithms. Our implementation of TAILOR is open-sourced at https://github.com/jifanz/TAILOR.
abstract_zh: 标签效率已成为深度学习应用中越来越重要的目标。主动学习的目的是减少训练深度网络所需的标记示例的数量，但主动学习算法的经验性能在不同的数据集和应用程序中可能会有很大差异。很难提前知道哪种主动学习策略在给定的应用中表现良好或最好。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了第一个深度主动学习的自适应算法选择策略。对于任何未标记的数据集，我们的（元）算法 TAILOR（汤普森主动学习算法选择）会迭代地、自适应地在一组候选主动学习算法中进行选择。 TAILOR 使用新颖的奖励函数来收集类别平衡的示例。多类和多标签应用中的大量实验证明了 TAILOR 在实现与最佳候选算法相当或更好的准确度方面的有效性。我们的 TAILOR 实现是开源的，位于 https://github.com/jifanz/TAILOR。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为TAILOR的自适应算法选择策略，用于解决深度主动学习中数据集不平衡的问题。
AI.motivation: 主动学习算法在不同数据集和应用场景中的表现差异显著，难以预先确定最佳策略，尤其在类别不平衡的情况下更需有效方法。
AI.method: TAILOR通过迭代选择候选主动学习算法，并设计新颖的奖励函数以收集类别平衡的样本，实现自适应算法选择。
AI.result: 在多类别和多标签任务中，TAILOR的准确率与最佳候选算法相当或更优，且代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: TAILOR有效解决了深度主动学习中算法选择难题，为处理不平衡数据提供了实用方案。

ai_content.conclusion: TAILOR有效解决了深度主动学习中算法选择难题，为处理不平衡数据提供了实用方案。

Our implementation of TAILOR is open-sourced at https://github.com/jifanz/TAILOR. 标签效率已成为深度学习应用中越来越重要的目标。主动学习的目的是减少训练深度网络所需的标记示例的数量，但主动学习算法的经验性能在不同的数据集和应用程序中可能会有很大差异。很难提前知道哪种主动学习策略在给定的应用中表现良好或最好。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了第一个深度主动学习的自适应算法选择策略。对于任何未标记的数据集，我们的（元）算法 TAILOR（汤普森主动学习算法选择）会迭代地、自适应地在一组候选主动学习算法中进行选择。 TAILOR 使用新颖的奖励函数来收集类别平衡的示例。多类和多标签应用中的大量实验证明了 TAILOR 在实现与最佳候选算法相当或更好的准确度方面的有效性。我们的 TAILOR 实现是开源的，位于 https://github.com/jifanz/TAILOR。 本文提出了一种名为TAILOR的自适应算法选择策略，用于解决深度主动学习中数据集不平衡的问题。 Jifan Zhang,Shuai Shao,Saurabh Verma,Robert Nowak cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV

authors_text: Rajat Hebbar,Digbalay Bose,Krishna Somandepalli,Veena Vijai,Shrikanth Narayanan
title: A dataset for Audio-Visual Sound Event Detection in Movies
title_zh: 电影中视听声音事件检测的数据集
abstract: Audio event detection is a widely studied audio processing task, with applications ranging from self-driving cars to healthcare. In-the-wild datasets such as Audioset have propelled research in this field. However, many efforts typically involve manual annotation and verification, which is expensive to perform at scale. Movies depict various real-life and fictional scenarios which makes them a rich resource for mining a wide-range of audio events. In this work, we present a dataset of audio events called Subtitle-Aligned Movie Sounds (SAM-S). We use publicly-available closed-caption transcripts to automatically mine over 110K audio events from 430 movies. We identify three dimensions to categorize audio events: sound, source, quality, and present the steps involved to produce a final taxonomy of 245 sounds. We discuss the choices involved in generating the taxonomy, and also highlight the human-centered nature of sounds in our dataset. We establish a baseline performance for audio-only sound classification of 34.76% mean average precision and show that incorporating visual information can further improve the performance by about 5%. Data and code are made available for research at https://github.com/usc-sail/mica-subtitle-aligned-movie-sounds
summary: Audio event detection is a widely studied audio processing task, with applications ranging from self-driving cars to healthcare. In-the-wild datasets such as Audioset have propelled research in this field. However, many efforts typically involve manual annotation and verification, which is expensive to perform at scale. Movies depict various real-life and fictional scenarios which makes them a rich resource for mining a wide-range of audio events. In this work, we present a dataset of audio events called Subtitle-Aligned Movie Sounds (SAM-S). We use publicly-available closed-caption transcripts to automatically mine over 110K audio events from 430 movies. We identify three dimensions to categorize audio events: sound, source, quality, and present the steps involved to produce a final taxonomy of 245 sounds. We discuss the choices involved in generating the taxonomy, and also highlight the human-centered nature of sounds in our dataset. We establish a baseline performance for audio-only sound classification of 34.76% mean average precision and show that incorporating visual information can further improve the performance by about 5%. Data and code are made available for research at https://github.com/usc-sail/mica-subtitle-aligned-movie-sounds
abstract_zh: 音频事件检测是一项被广泛研究的音频处理任务，其应用范围从自动驾驶汽车到医疗保健。 Audioset 等野外数据集推动了该领域的研究。然而，许多工作通常涉及手动注释和验证，大规模执行的成本很高。电影描绘了各种现实生活和虚构场景，这使得它们成为挖掘各种音频事件的丰富资源。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个名为字幕对齐电影声音（SAM-S）的音频事件数据集。我们使用公开的隐藏式字幕脚本自动挖掘 430 部电影中超过 110K 的音频事件。我们确定了三个维度来对音频事件进行分类：声音、来源、质量，并介绍了生成 245 种声音的最终分类所涉及的步骤。我们讨论了生成分类法所涉及的选择，并强调了数据集中声音的以人为中心的本质。我们为纯音频声音分类建立了平均精度为 34.76% 的基线性能，并表明结合视觉信息可以进一步将性能提高约 5%。数据和代码可供研究 https://github.com/usc-sail/mica-subtitle-aligned-movie-sounds

AI.tldr: 本文提出一个名为SAM-S的电影音频事件检测数据集，通过自动挖掘电影字幕中的音频事件并构建分类体系，推动音频-视觉声事件检测研究。
AI.motivation: 现有音频事件数据集依赖人工标注成本高，而电影包含丰富的现实与虚构场景，可作为大规模音频事件挖掘的资源，需建立自动化标注方法降低研究成本。
AI.method: 利用公开电影字幕自动挖掘11万+音频事件，按声音类型、来源及质量三个维度构建包含245类声音的分类体系，并建立基线模型验证音频-视觉融合效果。
AI.result: 音频-only分类基线达到34.76% mAP，结合视觉信息后性能提升约5%，数据集与代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: SAM-S数据集为音频事件检测提供了新资源，验证了多模态融合优势，并强调了人类感知视角在声音分类中的重要性。

authors_text: Hongyan Gu,Chunxu Yang,Mohammad Haeri,Jing Wang,Shirley Tang,Wenzhong Yan,Shujin He,Christopher Kazu Williams,Shino Magaki,Xiang 'Anthony' Chen
title: Augmenting Pathologists with NaviPath: Design and Evaluation of a Human-AI Collaborative Navigation System
title_zh: 使用 NaviPath 增强病理学家能力：人机协作导航系统的设计和评估
comment: Accepted ACM CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI '23)
comments: Accepted ACM CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI '23)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) brings advancements to support pathologists in navigating high-resolution tumor images to search for pathology patterns of interest. However, existing AI-assisted tools have not realized this promised potential due to a lack of insight into pathology and HCI considerations for pathologists' navigation workflows in practice. We first conducted a formative study with six medical professionals in pathology to capture their navigation strategies. By incorporating our observations along with the pathologists' domain knowledge, we designed NaviPath -- a human-AI collaborative navigation system. An evaluation study with 15 medical professionals in pathology indicated that: (i) compared to the manual navigation, participants saw more than twice the number of pathological patterns in unit time with NaviPath, and (ii) participants achieved higher precision and recall against the AI and the manual navigation on average. Further qualitative analysis revealed that navigation was more consistent with NaviPath, which can improve the overall examination quality.
summary: Artificial Intelligence (AI) brings advancements to support pathologists in navigating high-resolution tumor images to search for pathology patterns of interest. However, existing AI-assisted tools have not realized this promised potential due to a lack of insight into pathology and HCI considerations for pathologists' navigation workflows in practice. We first conducted a formative study with six medical professionals in pathology to capture their navigation strategies. By incorporating our observations along with the pathologists' domain knowledge, we designed NaviPath -- a human-AI collaborative navigation system. An evaluation study with 15 medical professionals in pathology indicated that: (i) compared to the manual navigation, participants saw more than twice the number of pathological patterns in unit time with NaviPath, and (ii) participants achieved higher precision and recall against the AI and the manual navigation on average. Further qualitative analysis revealed that navigation was more consistent with NaviPath, which can improve the overall examination quality.
abstract_zh: 人工智能 (AI) 带来的进步可支持病理学家浏览高分辨率肿瘤图像以搜索感兴趣的病理模式。然而，由于缺乏对病理学的深入了解以及病理学家在实践中导航工作流程的人机交互考虑因素，现有的人工智能辅助工具尚未实现这一承诺的潜力。我们首先对六名病理学医学专业人士进行了一项形成性研究，以了解他们的导航策略。通过将我们的观察结果与病理学家的领域知识相结合，我们设计了 NaviPath——一个人机协作导航系统。一项对 15 名病理学医疗专业人员进行的评估研究表明：(i) 与手动导航相比，参与者在单位时间内使用 NaviPath 看到的病理模式数量增加了一倍以上，(ii) 参与者相对于人工智能和手动导航平均获得了更高的精确度和召回率。进一步的定性分析显示，导航与NaviPath更加一致，可以提高整体检查质量。

AI.tldr: 本文设计并评估了NaviPath系统，该系统通过人机协作帮助病理学家更高效地导航高分辨率肿瘤图像以发现病理模式。
AI.motivation: 现有AI辅助工具未能充分结合病理学专业需求和人机交互特性，导致在实际导航流程中未能发挥潜力。
AI.method: 通过六名病理专家的形成性研究获取导航策略，结合领域知识设计NaviPath系统，并在15名病理专家中进行评估实验。
AI.result: 与手动导航相比，参与者在单位时间内发现的病理模式数量增加两倍以上，且平均精确率和召回率更高；定性分析显示导航一致性提升。
AI.conclusion: NaviPath通过融合人类专家与AI优势，可有效提升病理检查的整体质量。

authors_text: Joseph Chee Chang,Amy X. Zhang,Jonathan Bragg,Andrew Head,Kyle Lo,Doug Downey,Daniel S. Weld
title: CiteSee: Augmenting Citations in Scientific Papers with Persistent and Personalized Historical Context
title_zh: CiteSee：通过持久且个性化的历史背景增加科学论文的引用
abstract: When reading a scholarly article, inline citations help researchers contextualize the current article and discover relevant prior work. However, it can be challenging to prioritize and make sense of the hundreds of citations encountered during literature reviews. This paper introduces CiteSee, a paper reading tool that leverages a user's publishing, reading, and saving activities to provide personalized visual augmentations and context around citations. First, CiteSee connects the current paper to familiar contexts by surfacing known citations a user had cited or opened. Second, CiteSee helps users prioritize their exploration by highlighting relevant but unknown citations based on saving and reading history. We conducted a lab study that suggests CiteSee is significantly more effective for paper discovery than three baselines. A field deployment study shows CiteSee helps participants keep track of their explorations and leads to better situational awareness and increased paper discovery via inline citation when conducting real-world literature reviews.
summary: When reading a scholarly article, inline citations help researchers contextualize the current article and discover relevant prior work. However, it can be challenging to prioritize and make sense of the hundreds of citations encountered during literature reviews. First, CiteSee connects the current paper to familiar contexts by surfacing known citations a user had cited or opened. Second, CiteSee helps users prioritize their exploration by highlighting relevant but unknown citations based on saving and reading history. We conducted a lab study that suggests CiteSee is significantly more effective for paper discovery than three baselines. A field deployment study shows CiteSee helps participants keep track of their explorations and leads to better situational awareness and increased paper discovery via inline citation when conducting real-world literature reviews.
abstract_zh: 在阅读学术文章时，内联引用可以帮助研究人员了解当前文章的背景并发现相关的先前工作。然而，对文献综述期间遇到的数百条引用进行优先排序并理解其意义可能具有挑战性。本文介绍了 CiteSee，这是一种论文阅读工具，它利用用户的发布、阅读和保存活动来提供个性化的视觉增强和引文上下文。首先，CiteSee 通过显示用户引用或打开的已知引文，将当前论文与熟悉的上下文联系起来。其次，CiteSee 根据保存和阅读历史记录突出显示相关但未知的引文，帮助用户优先考虑其探索。我们进行的一项实验室研究表明，CiteSee 在论文发现方面比三个基线更有效。一项现场部署研究表明，CiteSee 可以帮助参与者跟踪他们的探索，并在进行现实世界文献综述时通过内联引用提高态势感知并增加论文发现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CiteSee工具，通过个性化历史上下文增强科学论文中的引用信息，帮助研究人员更高效地进行文献回顾。
AI.motivation: 在文献回顾过程中，研究人员面临大量引用信息难以优先排序和理解的挑战，传统方法无法有效整合用户历史行为提供个性化支持。
AI.method: 基于用户发表、阅读和保存行为数据，CiteSee通过可视化增强技术，将已知引用与未知但相关引用进行区分，利用历史记录优先展示潜在有价值文献。
AI.result: 实验室研究显示CiteSee显著优于三个基准工具，实地部署验证其能提升用户探索效率、情境感知能力和论文发现率。
AI.conclusion: CiteSee通过整合用户历史行为与引用上下文，有效解决了文献回顾中引用信息过载问题，为学术研究提供了智能化辅助方案。
