authors_text: Lauren Nicole DeLong,Ramon Fernández Mir,Jacques D. Fleuriot
title: Neurosymbolic AI for Reasoning over Knowledge Graphs: A Survey
title_zh: 用于知识图推理的神经符号人工智能：一项调查
comment: 21 pages, 6 figures, 2 tables, currently under review. Corresponding GitHub page here: https://github.com/NeSymGraphs. Revised in February 2024 according to major revisions, again in May 2024 according to minor revisions
comments: 21 pages, 6 figures, 2 tables, currently under review. Corresponding GitHub page here: https://github.com/NeSymGraphs. Revised in February 2024 according to major revisions, again in May 2024 according to minor revisions
abstract: Neurosymbolic AI is an increasingly active area of research that combines symbolic reasoning methods with deep learning to leverage their complementary benefits. As knowledge graphs are becoming a popular way to represent heterogeneous and multi-relational data, methods for reasoning on graph structures have attempted to follow this neurosymbolic paradigm. Traditionally, such approaches have utilized either rule-based inference or generated representative numerical embeddings from which patterns could be extracted. However, several recent studies have attempted to bridge this dichotomy to generate models that facilitate interpretability, maintain competitive performance, and integrate expert knowledge. Therefore, we survey methods that perform neurosymbolic reasoning tasks on knowledge graphs and propose a novel taxonomy by which we can classify them. Specifically, we propose three major categories: (1) logically-informed embedding approaches, (2) embedding approaches with logical constraints, and (3) rule learning approaches. Alongside the taxonomy, we provide a tabular overview of the approaches and links to their source code, if available, for more direct comparison. Finally, we discuss the unique characteristics and limitations of these methods, then propose several prospective directions toward which this field of research could evolve.
summary: Neurosymbolic AI is an increasingly active area of research that combines symbolic reasoning methods with deep learning to leverage their complementary benefits. As knowledge graphs are becoming a popular way to represent heterogeneous and multi-relational data, methods for reasoning on graph structures have attempted to follow this neurosymbolic paradigm. Traditionally, such approaches have utilized either rule-based inference or generated representative numerical embeddings from which patterns could be extracted. However, several recent studies have attempted to bridge this dichotomy to generate models that facilitate interpretability, maintain competitive performance, and integrate expert knowledge. Therefore, we survey methods that perform neurosymbolic reasoning tasks on knowledge graphs and propose a novel taxonomy by which we can classify them. Specifically, we propose three major categories: (1) logically-informed embedding approaches, (2) embedding approaches with logical constraints, and (3) rule learning approaches. Alongside the taxonomy, we provide a tabular overview of the approaches and links to their source code, if available, for more direct comparison. Finally, we discuss the unique characteristics and limitations of these methods, then propose several prospective directions toward which this field of research could evolve.
abstract_zh: 神经符号人工智能是一个日益活跃的研究领域，它将符号推理方法与深度学习相结合，以充分利用它们的互补优势。随着知识图成为表示异构和多关系数据的流行方式，图结构的推理方法试图遵循这种神经符号范式。传统上，此类方法要么利用基于规则的推理，要么生成可从中提取模式的代表性数字嵌入。然而，最近的几项研究试图弥合这种二分法，以生成促进可解释性、保持竞争绩效和整合专业知识的模型。因此，我们调查了在知识图上执行神经符号推理任务的方法，并提出了一种新的分类法，我们可以通过它对它们进行分类。具体来说，我们提出了三个主要类别：（1）逻辑知情的嵌入方法，（2）具有逻辑约束的嵌入方法，以及（3）规则学习方法。除了分类法之外，我们还提供了这些方法的表格概述以及源代码的链接（如果有），以便进行更直接的比较。最后，我们讨论这些方法的独特特征和局限性，然后提出该研究领域可以发展的几个前瞻性方向。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了神经符号AI在知识图谱推理领域的研究进展，提出了一种新的分类方法。
AI.motivation: 传统知识图谱推理方法存在可解释性差或性能不足的缺陷，需融合符号推理与深度学习的优势。
AI.method: 提出三类神经符号推理方法：逻辑引导嵌入、带约束嵌入和规则学习，并建立方法对比表格。
AI.result: 系统梳理了现有方法的特性与局限，总结了不同技术路线的适用场景与性能表现。
AI.conclusion: 未来研究应提升模型可扩展性，探索与强化学习等技术的结合，并加强实际应用验证。

authors_text: Austin P. Wright,Peter Nemere,Adrian Galvin,Duen Horng Chau,Scott Davidoff
title: Lessons from the Development of an Anomaly Detection Interface on the Mars Perseverance Rover using the ISHMAP Framework
title_zh: 使用 ISHMAP 框架在毅力号火星车上开发异常检测界面的经验教训
abstract: While anomaly detection stands among the most important and valuable problems across many scientific domains, anomaly detection research often focuses on AI methods that can lack the nuance and interpretability so critical to conducting scientific inquiry. In this application paper we present the results of utilizing an alternative approach that situates the mathematical framing of machine learning based anomaly detection within a participatory design framework. In a collaboration with NASA scientists working with the PIXL instrument studying Martian planetary geochemistry as a part of the search for extra-terrestrial life; we report on over 18 months of in-context user research and co-design to define the key problems NASA scientists face when looking to detect and interpret spectral anomalies. We address these problems and develop a novel spectral anomaly detection toolkit for PIXL scientists that is highly accurate while maintaining strong transparency to scientific interpretation. We also describe outcomes from a yearlong field deployment of the algorithm and associated interface. Finally we introduce a new design framework which we developed through the course of this collaboration for co-creating anomaly detection algorithms: Iterative Semantic Heuristic Modeling of Anomalous Phenomena (ISHMAP), which provides a process for scientists and researchers to produce natively interpretable anomaly detection models. This work showcases an example of successfully bridging methodologies from AI and HCI within a scientific domain, and provides a resource in ISHMAP which may be used by other researchers and practitioners looking to partner with other scientific teams to achieve better science through more effective and interpretable anomaly detection tools.

We address these problems and develop a novel spectral anomaly detection toolkit for PIXL scientists that is highly accurate while maintaining strong transparency to scientific interpretation. Finally we introduce a new design framework which we developed through the course of this collaboration for co-creating anomaly detection algorithms: Iterative Semantic Heuristic Modeling of Anomalous Phenomena (ISHMAP), which provides a process for scientists and researchers to produce natively interpretable anomaly detection models. This work showcases an example of successfully bridging methodologies from AI and HCI within a scientific domain, and provides a resource in ISHMAP which may be used by other researchers and practitioners looking to partner with other scientific teams to achieve better science through more effective and interpretable anomaly detection tools.
abstract_zh: 虽然异常检测是许多科学领域中最重要和最有价值的问题之一，但异常检测研究通常侧重于人工智能方法，这些方法可能缺乏对进行科学探究至关重要的细微差别和可解释性。在这篇应用论文中，我们展示了利用另一种方法的结果，该方法将基于机器学习的异常检测的数学框架置于参与式设计框架内。与 NASA 科学家合作，使用 PIXL 仪器研究火星行星地球化学，作为寻找外星生命的一部分；我们报告了超过 18 个月的背景用户研究和协同设计，以确定 NASA 科学家在检测和解释光谱异常时面临的关键问题。我们解决了这些问题，并为 PIXL 科学家开发了一种新型光谱异常检测工具包，该工具包高度准确，同时保持科学解释的高度透明度。我们还描述了算法和相关接口长达一年的现场部署的结果。最后，我们介绍了一个新的设计框架，该框架是我们在此次合作过程中开发的，用于共同创建异常检测算法：异常现象的迭代语义启发式建模（ISHMAP），它为科学家和研究人员提供了一个生成本机可解释的异常检测模型的过程。这项工作展示了在科学领域内成功桥接人工智能和人机交互方法论的例子，并在 ISHMAP 中提供了资源，其他研究人员和从业者可以使用该资源，寻求与其他科学团队合作，通过更有效和可解释的异常检测工具来实现更好的科学。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了利用ISHMAP框架为火星毅力号探测器开发异常检测接口的过程，结合NASA科学家的参与式设计，解决光谱异常检测与解释问题。
AI.motivation: 传统AI异常检测方法缺乏科学解释性，而科学探究需高透明度工具，需通过跨学科合作提升检测效果。
AI.method: 采用参与式设计框架，联合NASA科学家进行18个月用户研究与共设计，结合机器学习与领域知识构建ISHMAP框架，开发可解释的光谱异常检测工具。
AI.result: 开发出高精度且透明的异常检测工具，完成一年实地部署验证；提出ISHMAP框架，促进AI与HCI方法融合，为科学领域提供新资源。
AI.conclusion: 成功整合AI与HCI方法，通过ISHMAP框架为科学团队提供可解释的异常检测方案，推动更有效的科学发现合作模式。

This work showcases an example of successfully bridging methodologies from AI and HCI within a scientific domain, and provides a resource in ISHMAP which may be used by other researchers and practitioners looking to partner with other scientific teams to achieve better science through more effective and interpretable anomaly detection tools. 虽然异常检测是许多科学领域中最重要和最有价值的问题之一，但异常检测研究通常侧重于人工智能方法，这些方法可能缺乏对进行科学探究至关重要的细微差别和可解释性。在这篇应用论文中，我们展示了利用另一种方法的结果，该方法将基于机器学习的异常检测的数学框架置于参与式设计框架内。与 NASA 科学家合作，使用 PIXL 仪器研究火星行星地球化学，作为寻找外星生命的一部分；我们报告了超过 18 个月的背景用户研究和协同设计，以确定 NASA 科学家在检测和解释光谱异常时面临的关键问题。我们解决了这些问题，并为 PIXL 科学家开发了一种新型光谱异常检测工具包，该工具包高度准确，同时保持科学解释的高度透明度。我们还描述了算法和相关接口长达一年的现场部署的结果。最后，我们介绍了一个新的设计框架，该框架是我们在此次合作过程中开发的，用于共同创建异常检测算法：异常现象的迭代语义启发式建模（ISHMAP），它为科学家和研究人员提供了一个生成本机可解释的异常检测模型的过程。这项工作展示了在科学领域内成功桥接人工智能和人机交互方法论的例子，并在 ISHMAP 中提供了资源，其他研究人员和从业者可以使用该资源，寻求与其他科学团队合作，通过更有效和可解释的异常检测工具来实现更好的科学。 本文介绍了利用ISHMAP框架为火星毅力号探测器开发异常检测接口的过程，结合NASA科学家的参与式设计，解决光谱异常检测与解释问题。 Austin P. Wright,Peter Nemere,Adrian Galvin,Duen Horng Chau,Scott Davidoff cs.HC,cs.CE,cs.LG

authors_text: Ningli Xu,Rongjun Qin,Shuang Song
title: Point Cloud Registration for LiDAR and Photogrammetric Data: a Critical Synthesis and Performance Analysis on Classic and Deep Learning Algorithms
title_zh: LiDAR 和摄影测量数据的点云配准：经典和深度学习算法的关键综合和性能分析
comment: 7 figures
comments: 7 figures
abstract: Recent advances in computer vision and deep learning have shown promising performance in estimating rigid/similarity transformation between unregistered point clouds of complex objects and scenes. However, their performances are mostly evaluated using a limited number of datasets from a single sensor (e.g. Kinect or RealSense cameras), lacking a comprehensive overview of their applicability in photogrammetric 3D mapping scenarios. In this work, we provide a comprehensive review of the state-of-the-art (SOTA) point cloud registration methods, where we analyze and evaluate these methods using a diverse set of point cloud data from indoor to satellite sources. The quantitative analysis allows for exploring the strengths, applicability, challenges, and future trends of these methods. In contrast to existing analysis works that introduce point cloud registration as a holistic process, our experimental analysis is based on its inherent two-step process to better comprehend these approaches including feature/keypoint-based initial coarse registration and dense fine registration through cloud-to-cloud (C2C) optimization. More than ten methods, including classic hand-crafted, deep-learning-based feature correspondence, and robust C2C methods were tested. We observed that the success rate of most of the algorithms are fewer than 40% over the datasets we tested and there are still are large margin of improvement upon existing algorithms concerning 3D sparse corresopondence search, and the ability to register point clouds with complex geometry and occlusions. With the evaluated statistics on three datasets, we conclude the best-performing methods for each step and provide our recommendations, and outlook future efforts.
summary: Recent advances in computer vision and deep learning have shown promising performance in estimating rigid/similarity transformation between unregistered point clouds of complex objects and scenes. However, their performances are mostly evaluated using a limited number of datasets from a single sensor (e.g. Kinect or RealSense cameras), lacking a comprehensive overview of their applicability in photogrammetric 3D mapping scenarios. In this work, we provide a comprehensive review of the state-of-the-art (SOTA) point cloud registration methods, where we analyze and evaluate these methods using a diverse set of point cloud data from indoor to satellite sources. The quantitative analysis allows for exploring the strengths, applicability, challenges, and future trends of these methods. In contrast to existing analysis works that introduce point cloud registration as a holistic process, our experimental analysis is based on its inherent two-step process to better comprehend these approaches including feature/keypoint-based initial coarse registration and dense fine registration through cloud-to-cloud (C2C) optimization. More than ten methods, including classic hand-crafted, deep-learning-based feature correspondence, and robust C2C methods were tested. We observed that the success rate of most of the algorithms are fewer than 40% over the datasets we tested and there are still are large margin of improvement upon existing algorithms concerning 3D sparse corresopondence search, and the ability to register point clouds with complex geometry and occlusions. With the evaluated statistics on three datasets, we conclude the best-performing methods for each step and provide our recommendations, and outlook future efforts.
abstract_zh: 计算机视觉和深度学习的最新进展在估计复杂对象和场景的未配准点云之间的刚性/相似性变换方面表现出了良好的性能。然而，它们的性能大多是使用来自单个传感器（例如 Kinect 或 RealSense 相机）的有限数量的数据集来评估的，缺乏对其在摄影测量 3D 映射场景中的适用性的全面概述。在这项工作中，我们对最先进的 (SOTA) 点云配准方法进行了全面回顾，其中我们使用从室内到卫星源的各种点云数据集来分析和评估这些方法。定量分析可以探索这些方法的优势、适用性、挑战和未来趋势。与将点云配准作为整体过程引入的现有分析工作相比，我们的实验分析基于其固有的两步过程，以更好地理解这些方法，包括基于特征/关键点的初始粗配准和通过云到云（C2C）优化的密集精细配准。测试了十多种方法，包括经典的手工制作、基于深度学习的特征对应和稳健的 C2C 方法。我们观察到，与我们测试的数据集相比，大多数算法的成功率不到 40%，并且在 3D 稀疏对应搜索以及配准具有复杂几何和遮挡的点云的能力方面，现有算法仍有很大的改进空间。通过对三个数据集的评估统计数据，我们总结出每个步骤的最佳表现方法，并提供我们的建议，并展望未来的工作。

AI.tldr: 本文系统评估了经典与深度学习算法在LiDAR和摄影测量点云配准中的性能，分析其在不同场景下的适用性与挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有研究多基于单一传感器数据集，缺乏对摄影测量3D测绘场景的全面性能评估，需探索方法在复杂几何与遮挡情况下的表现。
AI.method: 将配准过程分为两步（粗配准与细配准），测试十余种方法（含传统、深度学习及C2C优化算法），基于多源数据集进行定量分析。
AI.result: 多数算法成功率低于40%，现有方法在稀疏对应搜索及复杂几何/遮挡场景下仍有显著改进空间。
AI.conclusion: 推荐各步骤最佳方法，未来需聚焦稀疏对应优化与复杂场景适应性提升，以推动点云配准技术发展。

authors_text: Zhentao Huang,Yukun Shi,Minglun Gong
title: Visibility-Aware Pixelwise View Selection for Multi-View Stereo Matching
title_zh: 用于多视图立体匹配的可见性感知像素视图选择
comment: 8 pages
comments: 8 pages
abstract: The performance of PatchMatch-based multi-view stereo algorithms depends heavily on the source views selected for computing matching costs. Instead of modeling the visibility of different views, most existing approaches handle occlusions in an ad-hoc manner. To address this issue, we propose a novel visibility-guided pixelwise view selection scheme in this paper. It progressively refines the set of source views to be used for each pixel in the reference view based on visibility information provided by already validated solutions. In addition, the Artificial Multi-Bee Colony (AMBC) algorithm is employed to search for optimal solutions for different pixels in parallel. Inter-colony communication is performed both within the same image and among different images. Fitness rewards are added to validated and propagated solutions, effectively enforcing the smoothness of neighboring pixels and allowing better handling of textureless areas. Experimental results on the DTU dataset show our method achieves state-of-the-art performance among non-learning-based methods and retrieves more details in occluded and low-textured regions.
summary: The performance of PatchMatch-based multi-view stereo algorithms depends heavily on the source views selected for computing matching costs. Instead of modeling the visibility of different views, most existing approaches handle occlusions in an ad-hoc manner. To address this issue, we propose a novel visibility-guided pixelwise view selection scheme in this paper. It progressively refines the set of source views to be used for each pixel in the reference view based on visibility information provided by already validated solutions. In addition, the Artificial Multi-Bee Colony (AMBC) algorithm is employed to search for optimal solutions for different pixels in parallel. Inter-colony communication is performed both within the same image and among different images. Fitness rewards are added to validated and propagated solutions, effectively enforcing the smoothness of neighboring pixels and allowing better handling of textureless areas. Experimental results on the DTU dataset show our method achieves state-of-the-art performance among non-learning-based methods and retrieves more details in occluded and low-textured regions.
abstract_zh: 基于 PatchMatch 的多视图立体算法的性能在很大程度上取决于为计算匹配成本而选择的源视图。大多数现有方法不是对不同视图的可见性进行建模，而是以临时方式处理遮挡。为了解决这个问题，我们在本文中提出了一种新颖的可见性引导像素视图选择方案。它根据已验证的解决方案提供的可见性信息，逐步细化用于参考视图中每个像素的源视图集。此外，采用人工多蜂群（AMBC）算法并行搜索不同像素的最优解。群体间的交流既可以在同一图像内进行，也可以在不同图像之间进行。健身奖励被添加到经过验证和传播的解决方案中，有效地增强了相邻像素的平滑度，并允许更好地处理无纹理区域。 DTU 数据集上的实验结果表明，我们的方法在非基于学习的方法中实现了最先进的性能，并在遮挡和低纹理区域中检索了更多细节。

AI.tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于可见性信息的像素级视图选择方法，以提高多视角立体匹配的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的基于PatchMatch的多视角立体算法在选择源视图时，通常以一种启发式的方式处理遮挡问题，而没有充分考虑不同视图的可见性信息。因此，需要一种更有效的方法来选择源视图以提高匹配精度。
AI.method: 论文提出了一种基于可见性的像素级视图选择方案，根据已验证的解提供的可见性信息逐步优化每个像素的源视图集。同时，采用人工多蜂群算法（AMBC）并行搜索不同像素的最优解，并在图像内部和不同图像之间进行蜂群间通信。通过增加对已验证和传播解的适应度奖励，增强邻近像素的平滑性，从而更好地处理纹理较少的区域。
AI.result: 在DTU数据集上的实验表明，该方法在非学习方法中达到了最先进的性能，并在遮挡和低纹理区域中恢复了更多细节。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过引入可见性信息和优化视图选择，有效提升了多视角立体匹配的性能，特别是在处理遮挡和低纹理区域方面表现突出。

Instead of modeling the visibility of different views, most existing approaches handle occlusions in an ad-hoc manner. To address this issue, we propose a novel visibility-guided pixelwise view selection scheme in this paper. It progressively refines the set of source views to be used for each pixel in the reference view based on visibility information provided by already validated solutions. In addition, the Artificial Multi-Bee Colony (AMBC) algorithm is employed to search for optimal solutions for different pixels in parallel. Inter-colony communication is performed both within the same image and among different images. Fitness rewards are added to validated and propagated solutions, effectively enforcing the smoothness of neighboring pixels and allowing better handling of textureless areas. Experimental results on the DTU dataset show our method achieves state-of-the-art performance among non-learning-based methods and retrieves more details in occluded and low-textured regions. 基于 PatchMatch 的多视图立体算法的性能在很大程度上取决于为计算匹配成本而选择的源视图。大多数现有方法不是对不同视图的可见性进行建模，而是以临时方式处理遮挡。为了解决这个问题，我们在本文中提出了一种新颖的可见性引导像素视图选择方案。它根据已验证的解决方案提供的可见性信息，逐步细化用于参考视图中每个像素的源视图集。此外，采用人工多蜂群（AMBC）算法并行搜索不同像素的最优解。群体间的交流既可以在同一图像内进行，也可以在不同图像之间进行。健身奖励被添加到经过验证和传播的解决方案中，有效地增强了相邻像素的平滑度，并允许更好地处理无纹理区域。 DTU 数据集上的实验结果表明，我们的方法在非基于学习的方法中实现了最先进的性能，并在遮挡和低纹理区域中检索了更多细节。 该论文提出了一种基于可见性信息的像素级视图选择方法，以提高多视角立体匹配的性能。 Zhentao Huang,Yukun Shi,Minglun Gong cs.CV

authors_text: Ernestine Großmann,Jonas Sauer,Christian Schulz,Patrick Steil
title: Arc-Flags Meet Trip-Based Public Transit Routing
title_zh: Arc-Flags 满足基于行程的公共交通路线
abstract: We present Arc-Flag TB, a journey planning algorithm for public transit networks which combines Trip-Based Public Transit Routing (TB) with the Arc-Flags speedup technique. Compared to previous attempts to apply Arc-Flags to public transit networks, which saw limited success, our approach uses stronger pruning rules to reduce the search space. Our experiments show that Arc-Flag TB achieves a speedup of up to two orders of magnitude over TB, offering query times of less than a millisecond even on large countrywide networks. Compared to the state-of-the-art speedup technique Trip-Based Public Transit Routing Using Condensed Search Trees (TB-CST), our algorithm achieves similar query times but requires significantly less additional memory. Other state-of-the-art algorithms which achieve even faster query times, e.g., Public Transit Labeling, require enormous memory usage. In contrast, Arc-Flag TB offers a tradeoff between query performance and memory usage due to the fact that the number of regions in the network partition required by our algorithm is a configurable parameter. We also identify an issue in the transfer precomputation of TB that affects both TB-CST and Arc-Flag TB, leading to incorrect answers for some queries. This has not been previously recognized by the author of TB-CST. We provide discussion on how to resolve this issue in the future. Currently, Arc-Flag TB answers 1-6% of queries incorrectly, compared to over 20% for TB-CST on some networks.

Compared to previous attempts to apply Arc-Flags to public transit networks, which saw limited success, our approach uses stronger pruning rules to reduce the search space. Our experiments show that Arc-Flag TB achieves a speedup of up to two orders of magnitude over TB, offering query times of less than a millisecond even on large countrywide networks. Compared to the state-of-the-art speedup technique Trip-Based Public Transit Routing Using Condensed Search Trees (TB-CST), our algorithm achieves similar query times but requires significantly less additional memory. Other state-of-the-art algorithms which achieve even faster query times, e.g., Public Transit Labeling, require enormous memory usage. In contrast, Arc-Flag TB offers a tradeoff between query performance and memory usage due to the fact that the number of regions in the network partition required by our algorithm is a configurable parameter. We also identify an issue in the transfer precomputation of TB that affects both TB-CST and Arc-Flag TB, leading to incorrect answers for some queries. This has not been previously recognized by the author of TB-CST. We provide discussion on how to resolve this issue in the future. Currently, Arc-Flag TB answers 1-6% of queries incorrectly, compared to over 20% for TB-CST on some networks.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 Arc-Flag TB，这是一种公共交通网络的旅程规划算法，它将基于行程的公共交通路线 (TB) 与 Arc-Flags 加速技术相结合。与之前将 Arc-Flags 应用到公共交通网络的尝试相比，该方法取得了有限的成功，我们的方法使用更强的修剪规则来减少搜索空间。我们的实验表明，Arc-Flag TB 的速度比 TB 提高了两个数量级，即使在大型全国网络上也能提供不到一毫秒的查询时间。与最先进的加速技术使用压缩搜索树的基于行程的公共交通路由 (TB-CST) 相比，我们的算法实现了相似的查询时间，但需要的额外内存明显更少。其他最先进的算法可以实现更快的查询时间，例如公共交通标签，需要大量的内存使用。相比之下，Arc-Flag TB 提供了查询性能和内存使用之间的权衡，因为我们的算法所需的网络分区中的区域数量是一个可配置的参数。我们还发现了 TB 传输预计算中的一个问题，该问题同时影响 TB-CST 和 Arc-Flag TB，导致某些查询的答案不正确。 TB-CST 的作者之前并未认识到这一点。我们就未来如何解决这个问题进行讨论。目前，Arc-Flag TB 错误回答了 1-6% 的查询，而某些网络上的 TB-CST 错误回答了 20% 以上。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合弧标志（Arc-Flags）与基于行程的公共交通路由（TB）的算法Arc-Flag TB，旨在提升公共交通网络中的路径规划效率。
AI.motivation: 现有基于Arc-Flags的公共交通路由方法效果有限，而TB-CST等技术虽查询速度快但内存消耗大，需寻求性能与内存的平衡方案。
AI.method: 通过强化剪枝规则优化搜索空间，结合弧标志分区技术与基于行程的路由，并将网络分区数量设为可配置参数以调节性能与内存占用。
AI.result: 在大规模网络中实现最高达100倍的加速，查询时间低于1毫秒，内存消耗显著低于TB-CST，但存在1-6%的错误率（TB-CST错误率超20%）。
AI.conclusion: Arc-Flag TB在查询性能与内存间取得平衡，但需解决转移预计算中的错误问题，未来需进一步优化算法准确性。

authors_text: Daniel Nyga,Mareike Picklum,Tom Schierenbeck,Michael Beetz
title: Joint Probability Trees
title_zh: 联合概率树
abstract: We introduce Joint Probability Trees (JPT), a novel approach that makes learning of and reasoning about joint probability distributions tractable for practical applications. JPTs support both symbolic and subsymbolic variables in a single hybrid model, and they do not rely on prior knowledge about variable dependencies or families of distributions. JPT representations build on tree structures that partition the problem space into relevant subregions that are elicited from the training data instead of postulating a rigid dependency model prior to learning. Learning and reasoning scale linearly in JPTs, and the tree structure allows white-box reasoning about any posterior probability $P(Q|E)$, such that interpretable explanations can be provided for any inference result. Our experiments showcase the practical applicability of JPTs in high-dimensional heterogeneous probability spaces with millions of training samples, making it a promising alternative to classic probabilistic graphical models.
summary: We introduce Joint Probability Trees (JPT), a novel approach that makes learning of and reasoning about joint probability distributions tractable for practical applications. JPTs support both symbolic and subsymbolic variables in a single hybrid model, and they do not rely on prior knowledge about variable dependencies or families of distributions. JPT representations build on tree structures that partition the problem space into relevant subregions that are elicited from the training data instead of postulating a rigid dependency model prior to learning. Learning and reasoning scale linearly in JPTs, and the tree structure allows white-box reasoning about any posterior probability $P(Q|E)$, such that interpretable explanations can be provided for any inference result. Our experiments showcase the practical applicability of JPTs in high-dimensional heterogeneous probability spaces with millions of training samples, making it a promising alternative to classic probabilistic graphical models.
abstract_zh: 我们引入联合概率树（JPT），这是一种新颖的方法，可以使联合概率分布的学习和推理在实际应用中变得易于处理。 JPT 在单个混合模型中支持符号变量和子符号变量，并且它们不依赖于有关变量依赖性或分布族的先验知识。 JPT 表示建立在树结构的基础上，将问题空间划分为从训练数据中得出的相关子区域，而不是在学习之前假设严格的依赖模型。 JPT 中的学习和推理呈线性扩展，并且树结构允许对任何后验概率 $P(Q|E)$ 进行白盒推理，从而可以为任​​何推理结果提供可解释的解释。我们的实验展示了 JPT 在具有数百万训练样本的高维异构概率空间中的实际适用性，使其成为经典概率图模型的有前途的替代方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出联合概率树（JPT），一种无需先验依赖假设即可高效学习和推理联合概率分布的混合模型方法。
AI.motivation: 传统概率图模型依赖人工设定变量依赖关系且难以处理高维异构数据，现有方法在可解释性与扩展性上存在局限。
AI.method: 构建基于树结构的混合模型，通过训练数据动态划分问题子空间，同时支持符号变量与次符号变量，实现线性扩展的学习与白盒推理。
AI.result: 实验验证JPT在百万级样本的高维异构概率空间中表现优异，可提供可解释的后验概率推理结果。
AI.conclusion: JPT为复杂概率建模提供了兼具可解释性与扩展性的新型解决方案，是传统概率图模型的有效替代方法。

authors_text: Pierre Tassel,Benjamin Kovács,Martin Gebser,Konstantin Schekotihin,Patrick Stöckermann,Georg Seidel
title: Semiconductor Fab Scheduling with Self-Supervised and Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 具有自我监督和强化学习的半导体工厂调度
abstract: Semiconductor manufacturing is a notoriously complex and costly multi-step process involving a long sequence of operations on expensive and quantity-limited equipment. Recent chip shortages and their impacts have highlighted the importance of semiconductors in the global supply chains and how reliant on those our daily lives are. Due to the investment cost, environmental impact, and time scale needed to build new factories, it is difficult to ramp up production when demand spikes. This work introduces a method to successfully learn to schedule a semiconductor manufacturing facility more efficiently using deep reinforcement and self-supervised learning. We propose the first adaptive scheduling approach to handle complex, continuous, stochastic, dynamic, modern semiconductor manufacturing models. Our method outperforms the traditional hierarchical dispatching strategies typically used in semiconductor manufacturing plants, substantially reducing each order's tardiness and time until completion. As a result, our method yields a better allocation of resources in the semiconductor manufacturing process.
summary: Semiconductor manufacturing is a notoriously complex and costly multi-step process involving a long sequence of operations on expensive and quantity-limited equipment. Recent chip shortages and their impacts have highlighted the importance of semiconductors in the global supply chains and how reliant on those our daily lives are. Due to the investment cost, environmental impact, and time scale needed to build new factories, it is difficult to ramp up production when demand spikes. This work introduces a method to successfully learn to schedule a semiconductor manufacturing facility more efficiently using deep reinforcement and self-supervised learning. We propose the first adaptive scheduling approach to handle complex, continuous, stochastic, dynamic, modern semiconductor manufacturing models. Our method outperforms the traditional hierarchical dispatching strategies typically used in semiconductor manufacturing plants, substantially reducing each order's tardiness and time until completion. As a result, our method yields a better allocation of resources in the semiconductor manufacturing process.
abstract_zh: 众所周知，半导体制造是一个极其复杂且成本高昂的多步骤过程，涉及在昂贵且数量有限的设备上进行的一系列操作。最近的芯片短缺及其影响凸显了半导体在全球供应链中的重要性以及我们日常生活对半导体的依赖程度。由于建设新工厂所需的投资成本、环境影响和时间规模，当需求激增时很难提高产量。这项工作介绍了一种使用深度强化和自我监督学习成功学习更有效地安排半导体制造设施的方法。我们提出了第一个自适应调度方法来处理复杂、连续、随机、动态的现代半导体制造模型。我们的方法优于半导体制造厂通常使用的传统分层调度策略，大大减少了每个订单的延迟和完成时间。因此，我们的方法可以在半导体制造过程中更好地分配资源。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合深度强化学习与自监督学习的半导体制造工厂调度方法，以提升生产效率。
AI.motivation: 半导体制造过程复杂且成本高昂，传统调度策略难以应对动态需求变化，导致资源分配效率低下。
AI.method: 采用深度强化学习与自监督学习相结合的自适应调度框架，处理现代半导体制造中的复杂动态模型。
AI.result: 该方法显著降低订单延迟与完成时间，优于传统分层调度策略，实现更优资源分配。
AI.conclusion: 新型调度方法有效提升半导体制造效率，为复杂工业场景提供可行优化方案。

search_text: Semiconductor Fab Scheduling with Self-Supervised and Reinforcement Learning 具有自我监督和强化学习的半导体工厂调度 Semiconductor manufacturing is a notoriously complex and costly multi-step process involving a long sequence of operations on expensive and quantity-limited equipment. Recent chip shortages and their impacts have highlighted the importance of semiconductors in the global supply chains and how reliant on those our daily lives are. Due to the investment cost, environmental impact, and time scale needed to build new factories, it is difficult to ramp up production when demand spikes. This work introduces a method to successfully learn to schedule a semiconductor manufacturing facility more efficiently using deep reinforcement and self-supervised learning. We propose the first adaptive scheduling approach to handle complex, continuous, stochastic, dynamic, modern semiconductor manufacturing models. Our method outperforms the traditional hierarchical dispatching strategies typically used in semiconductor manufacturing plants, substantially reducing each order's tardiness and time until completion. As a result, our method yields a better allocation of resources in the semiconductor manufacturing process. 众所周知，半导体制造是一个极其复杂且成本高昂的多步骤过程，涉及在昂贵且数量有限的设备上进行的一系列操作。最近的芯片短缺及其影响凸显了半导体在全球供应链中的重要性以及我们日常生活对半导体的依赖程度。由于建设新工厂所需的投资成本、环境影响和时间规模，当需求激增时很难提高产量。这项工作介绍了一种使用深度强化和自我监督学习成功学习更有效地安排半导体制造设施的方法。我们提出了第一个自适应调度方法来处理复杂、连续、随机、动态的现代半导体制造模型。我们的方法优于半导体制造厂通常使用的传统分层调度策略，大大减少了每个订单的延迟和完成时间。因此，我们的方法可以在半导体制造过程中更好地分配资源。 本文提出一种结合深度强化学习与自监督学习的半导体制造工厂调度方法，以提升生产效率。 Pierre Tassel,Benjamin Kovács,Martin Gebser,Konstantin Schekotihin,Patrick Stöckermann,Georg Seidel cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: Meifang Zeng,Ke Li,Bingchuan Jiang,Liujuan Cao,Hui Li
title: Practical Cross-System Shilling Attacks with Limited Access to Data
title_zh: 数据访问受限的实用跨系统先令攻击
comment: AAAI 2023
comments: AAAI 2023
abstract: In shilling attacks, an adversarial party injects a few fake user profiles into a Recommender System (RS) so that the target item can be promoted or demoted. Although much effort has been devoted to developing shilling attack methods, we find that existing approaches are still far from practical. In this paper, we analyze the properties a practical shilling attack method should have and propose a new concept of Cross-system Attack. With the idea of Cross-system Attack, we design a Practical Cross-system Shilling Attack (PC-Attack) framework that requires little information about the victim RS model and the target RS data for conducting attacks. PC-Attack is trained to capture graph topology knowledge from public RS data in a self-supervised manner. Then, it is fine-tuned on a small portion of target data that is easy to access to construct fake profiles. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the superiority of PC-Attack over state-of-the-art baselines. Our implementation of PC-Attack is available at https://github.com/KDEGroup/PC-Attack.
summary: In shilling attacks, an adversarial party injects a few fake user profiles into a Recommender System (RS) so that the target item can be promoted or demoted. Although much effort has been devoted to developing shilling attack methods, we find that existing approaches are still far from practical. In this paper, we analyze the properties a practical shilling attack method should have and propose a new concept of Cross-system Attack. With the idea of Cross-system Attack, we design a Practical Cross-system Shilling Attack (PC-Attack) framework that requires little information about the victim RS model and the target RS data for conducting attacks. PC-Attack is trained to capture graph topology knowledge from public RS data in a self-supervised manner. Then, it is fine-tuned on a small portion of target data that is easy to access to construct fake profiles. Extensive experiments have demonstrated the superiority of PC-Attack over state-of-the-art baselines. Our implementation of PC-Attack is available at https://github.com/KDEGroup/PC-Attack.
abstract_zh: 在先令攻击中，敌方将一些虚假的用户配置文件注入推荐系统（RS）中，以便对目标项目进行升级或降级。尽管人们在开发先令攻击方法方面付出了很大的努力，但我们发现现有的方法仍然远未实用。在本文中，我们分析了实用的先令攻击方法应具备的属性，并提出了跨系统攻击的新概念。借助跨系统攻击的思想，我们设计了一个实用的跨系统先令攻击（PC-Attack）框架，该框架只需要很少的受害者 RS 模型和目标 RS 数据信息即可进行攻击。 PC-Attack 经过训练，可以以自我监督的方式从公共 RS 数据中捕获图拓扑知识。然后，它对一小部分易于访问的目标数据进行微调，以构建虚假配置文件。大量的实验证明了 PC-Attack 相对于最先进的基线的优越性。我们的 PC-Attack 实现可在 https://github.com/KDEGroup/PC-Attack 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种在数据访问受限情况下实现跨系统水军攻击的实用方法PC-Attack。
AI.motivation: 现有水军攻击方法依赖大量目标系统数据且难以实际应用，需设计信息需求更低的攻击框架。
AI.method: 通过自监督学习从公开推荐系统数据中提取图结构知识，结合少量目标数据微调生成伪造用户画像。
AI.result: 实验验证PC-Attack在多个基准数据集上优于现有最先进方法，攻击成功率显著提升。
AI.conclusion: PC-Attack为有限信息条件下的跨系统推荐系统攻击提供了有效解决方案。

authors_text: Christian Pehle,Luca Blessing,Elias Arnold,Eric Müller,Johannes Schemmel
title: Event-based Backpropagation for Analog Neuromorphic Hardware
title_zh: 模拟神经形态硬件的基于事件的反向传播
abstract: Neuromorphic computing aims to incorporate lessons from studying biological nervous systems in the design of computer architectures. While existing approaches have successfully implemented aspects of those computational principles, such as sparse spike-based computation, event-based scalable learning has remained an elusive goal in large-scale systems. However, only then the potential energy-efficiency advantages of neuromorphic systems relative to other hardware architectures can be realized during learning. We present our progress implementing the EventProp algorithm using the example of the BrainScaleS-2 analog neuromorphic hardware. Previous gradient-based approaches to learning used "surrogate gradients" and dense sampling of observables or were limited by assumptions on the underlying dynamics and loss functions. In contrast, our approach only needs spike time observations from the system while being able to incorporate other system observables, such as membrane voltage measurements, in a principled way. This leads to a one-order-of-magnitude improvement in the information efficiency of the gradient estimate, which would directly translate to corresponding energy efficiency improvements in an optimized hardware implementation. We present the theoretical framework for estimating gradients and results verifying the correctness of the estimation, as well as results on a low-dimensional classification task using the BrainScaleS-2 system. Building on this work has the potential to enable scalable gradient estimation in large-scale neuromorphic hardware as a continuous measurement of the system state would be prohibitive and energy-inefficient in such instances. It also suggests the feasibility of a full on-device implementation of the algorithm that would enable scalable, energy-efficient, event-based learning in large-scale analog neuromorphic hardware.
summary: Neuromorphic computing aims to incorporate lessons from studying biological nervous systems in the design of computer architectures. While existing approaches have successfully implemented aspects of those computational principles, such as sparse spike-based computation, event-based scalable learning has remained an elusive goal in large-scale systems. However, only then the potential energy-efficiency advantages of neuromorphic systems relative to other hardware architectures can be realized during learning. We present our progress implementing the EventProp algorithm using the example of the BrainScaleS-2 analog neuromorphic hardware. Previous gradient-based approaches to learning used "surrogate gradients" and dense sampling of observables or were limited by assumptions on the underlying dynamics and loss functions. In contrast, our approach only needs spike time observations from the system while being able to incorporate other system observables, such as membrane voltage measurements, in a principled way. This leads to a one-order-of-magnitude improvement in the information efficiency of the gradient estimate, which would directly translate to corresponding energy efficiency improvements in an optimized hardware implementation. We present the theoretical framework for estimating gradients and results verifying the correctness of the estimation, as well as results on a low-dimensional classification task using the BrainScaleS-2 system. Building on this work has the potential to enable scalable gradient estimation in large-scale neuromorphic hardware as a continuous measurement of the system state would be prohibitive and energy-inefficient in such instances. It also suggests the feasibility of a full on-device implementation of the algorithm that would enable scalable, energy-efficient, event-based learning in large-scale analog neuromorphic hardware.
abstract_zh: 神经形态计算旨在将生物神经系统研究的经验教训纳入计算机架构的设计中。虽然现有方法已经成功地实现了这些计算原理的各个方面，例如基于稀疏尖峰的计算，但基于事件的可扩展学习在大规模系统中仍然是一个难以实现的目标。然而，只有这样，神经形态系统相对于其他硬件架构的潜在能源效率优势才能在学习过程中得以实现。我们使用 BrainScaleS-2 模拟神经形态硬件的示例展示了我们实现 EventProp 算法的进展。以前基于梯度的学习方法使用“替代梯度”和可观测值的密集采样，或者受到对潜在动态和损失函数的假设的限制。相比之下，我们的方法只需要系统的尖峰时间观测，同时能够以原则性的方式合并其他系统观测值，例如膜电压测量。这导致梯度估计的信息效率提高一个数量级，这将直接转化为优化硬件实现中相应的能源效率提高。我们提出了估计梯度的理论框架和验证估计正确性的结果，以及使用 BrainScaleS-2 系统的低维分类任务的结果。在此工作的基础上，有可能在大规模神经形态硬件中实现可扩展的梯度估计，因为在这种情况下，对系统状态的连续测量将是令人望而却步且能源效率低下的。它还表明了该算法在设备上完整实现的可行性，该算法将在大规模模拟神经形态硬件中实现可扩展、节能、基于事件的学习。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于事件的反向传播算法（EventProp），用于在类脑模拟硬件（BrainScaleS-2）上实现高效的学习。
AI.motivation: 尽管神经形态计算在稀疏脉冲计算方面取得进展，但大规模系统中事件驱动的可扩展学习仍难以实现，限制了其能效优势的发挥。
AI.method: 通过仅利用系统中的脉冲时间观测，并结合膜电位等其他可观测变量，提出无需密集采样和简化动态假设的梯度估计框架。
AI.result: 实现了梯度估计的信息效率提升一个数量级，并在BrainScaleS-2硬件上验证了低维分类任务的正确性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为大规模神经形态硬件提供了可扩展的事件驱动学习方案，支持全芯片算法实现以提升能效。

result: 实现了梯度估计的信息效率提升一个数量级，并在BrainScaleS-2硬件上验证了低维分类任务的正确性。
conclusion: 该方法为大规模神经形态硬件提供了可扩展的事件驱动学习方案，支持全芯片算法实现以提升能效。
search_text: Event-based Backpropagation for Analog Neuromorphic Hardware 模拟神经形态硬件的基于事件的反向传播 Neuromorphic computing aims to incorporate lessons from studying biological nervous systems in the design of computer architectures. While existing approaches have successfully implemented aspects of those computational principles, such as sparse spike-based computation, event-based scalable learning has remained an elusive goal in large-scale systems. However, only then the potential energy-efficiency advantages of neuromorphic systems relative to other hardware architectures can be realized during learning. We present our progress implementing the EventProp algorithm using the example of the BrainScaleS-2 analog neuromorphic hardware. Previous gradient-based approaches to learning used "surrogate gradients" and dense sampling of observables or were limited by assumptions on the underlying dynamics and loss functions. In contrast, our approach only needs spike time observations from the system while being able to incorporate other system observables, such as membrane voltage measurements, in a principled way. This leads to a one-order-of-magnitude improvement in the information efficiency of the gradient estimate, which would directly translate to corresponding energy efficiency improvements in an optimized hardware implementation. We present the theoretical framework for estimating gradients and results verifying the correctness of the estimation, as well as results on a low-dimensional classification task using the BrainScaleS-2 system. Building on this work has the potential to enable scalable gradient estimation in large-scale neuromorphic hardware as a continuous measurement of the system state would be prohibitive and energy-inefficient in such instances. It also suggests the feasibility of a full on-device implementation of the algorithm that would enable scalable, energy-efficient, event-based learning in large-scale analog neuromorphic hardware. 神经形态计算旨在将生物神经系统研究的经验教训纳入计算机架构的设计中。虽然现有方法已经成功地实现了这些计算原理的各个方面，例如基于稀疏尖峰的计算，但基于事件的可扩展学习在大规模系统中仍然是一个难以实现的目标。然而，只有这样，神经形态系统相对于其他硬件架构的潜在能源效率优势才能在学习过程中得以实现。我们使用 BrainScaleS-2 模拟神经形态硬件的示例展示了我们实现 EventProp 算法的进展。以前基于梯度的学习方法使用“替代梯度”和可观测值的密集采样，或者受到对潜在动态和损失函数的假设的限制。相比之下，我们的方法只需要系统的尖峰时间观测，同时能够以原则性的方式合并其他系统观测值，例如膜电压测量。这导致梯度估计的信息效率提高一个数量级，这将直接转化为优化硬件实现中相应的能源效率提高。我们提出了估计梯度的理论框架和验证估计正确性的结果，以及使用 BrainScaleS-2 系统的低维分类任务的结果。在此工作的基础上，有可能在大规模神经形态硬件中实现可扩展的梯度估计，因为在这种情况下，对系统状态的连续测量将是令人望而却步且能源效率低下的。它还表明了该算法在设备上完整实现的可行性，该算法将在大规模模拟神经形态硬件中实现可扩展、节能、基于事件的学习。 本文提出了一种基于事件的反向传播算法（EventProp），用于在类脑模拟硬件（BrainScaleS-2）上实现高效的学习。 Christian Pehle,Luca Blessing,Elias Arnold,Eric Müller,Johannes Schemmel q-bio.NC,cs.NE

authors_text: Yuan Yang,Deepayan Sanyal,Joel Michelson,James Ainooson,Maithilee Kunda
title: Deep Non-Monotonic Reasoning for Visual Abstract Reasoning Tasks
title_zh: 视觉抽象推理任务的深度非单调推理
abstract: While achieving unmatched performance on many well-defined tasks, deep learning models have also been used to solve visual abstract reasoning tasks, which are relatively less well-defined, and have been widely used to measure human intelligence. However, current deep models struggle to match human abilities to solve such tasks with minimum data but maximum generalization. One limitation is that current deep learning models work in a monotonic way, i.e., treating different parts of the input in essentially fixed orderings, whereas people repeatedly observe and reason about the different parts of the visual stimuli until the reasoning process converges to a consistent conclusion, i.e., non-monotonic reasoning. This paper proposes a non-monotonic computational approach to solve visual abstract reasoning tasks. In particular, we implemented a deep learning model using this approach and tested it on the RAVEN dataset -- a dataset inspired by the Raven's Progressive Matrices test. Results show that the proposed approach is more effective than existing monotonic deep learning models, under strict experimental settings that represent a difficult variant of the RAVEN dataset problem.
summary: While achieving unmatched performance on many well-defined tasks, deep learning models have also been used to solve visual abstract reasoning tasks, which are relatively less well-defined, and have been widely used to measure human intelligence. However, current deep models struggle to match human abilities to solve such tasks with minimum data but maximum generalization. One limitation is that current deep learning models work in a monotonic way, i.e., treating different parts of the input in essentially fixed orderings, whereas people repeatedly observe and reason about the different parts of the visual stimuli until the reasoning process converges to a consistent conclusion, i.e., non-monotonic reasoning. This paper proposes a non-monotonic computational approach to solve visual abstract reasoning tasks. In particular, we implemented a deep learning model using this approach and tested it on the RAVEN dataset -- a dataset inspired by the Raven's Progressive Matrices test. Results show that the proposed approach is more effective than existing monotonic deep learning models, under strict experimental settings that represent a difficult variant of the RAVEN dataset problem.
abstract_zh: 深度学习模型在许多定义明确的任务上取得了无与伦比的性能的同时，也被用来解决定义相对不太明确的视觉抽象推理任务，并被广泛用于衡量人类智力。然而，当前的深度模型很难与人类能力相匹配，以最少的数据和最大的泛化能力来解决此类任务。一个限制是当前的深度学习模型以单调的方式工作，即以基本上固定的顺序处理输入的不同部分，而人们反复观察和推理视觉刺激的不同部分，直到推理过程收敛到一致的结论，即非单调推理。本文提出了一种非单调计算方法来解决视觉抽象推理任务。特别是，我们使用这种方法实现了一个深度学习模型，并在 RAVEN 数据集上进行了测试——这是一个受 Raven 渐进矩阵测试启发的数据集。结果表明，在代表 RAVEN 数据集问题的困难变体的严格实验设置下，所提出的方法比现有的单调深度学习模型更有效。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于非单调推理的深度学习方法，用于解决视觉抽象推理任务，并在RAVEN数据集上验证了其有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习模型在处理视觉抽象推理任务时表现不足，因其采用单调推理方式（固定输入顺序），而人类通过反复观察和非单调推理达成结论。
AI.method: 设计非单调计算框架，实现深度学习模型并在RAVEN数据集（模拟瑞文推理测试）上进行严格实验验证。
AI.result: 所提方法在RAVEN数据集困难变体中表现优于现有单调模型，证明非单调推理的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 非单调推理机制能提升模型在少样本条件下的泛化能力，为构建接近人类推理能力的AI提供了新方向。

authors_text: Subaveerapandiyan A,Vinoth A,Neelam Tiwary
title: Netizens, Academicians, and Information Professionals' Opinions About AI With Special Reference To ChatGPT
title_zh: 网民、院士和信息专业人士对人工智能的看法，特别提到ChatGPT
abstract: This study aims to understand the perceptions and opinions of academicians towards ChatGPT-3 by collecting and analyzing social media comments, and a survey was conducted with library and information science professionals. The research uses a content analysis method and finds that while ChatGPT-3 can be a valuable tool for research and writing, it is not 100% accurate and should be cross-checked. The study also finds that while some academicians may not accept ChatGPT-3, most are starting to accept it. The study is beneficial for academicians, content developers, and librarians.
summary: This study aims to understand the perceptions and opinions of academicians towards ChatGPT-3 by collecting and analyzing social media comments, and a survey was conducted with library and information science professionals. The research uses a content analysis method and finds that while ChatGPT-3 can be a valuable tool for research and writing, it is not 100% accurate and should be cross-checked. The study also finds that while some academicians may not accept ChatGPT-3, most are starting to accept it. The study is beneficial for academicians, content developers, and librarians.
abstract_zh: 本研究旨在通过收集和分析社交媒体评论，了解院士们对 ChatGPT-3 的看法和看法，并与图书馆和信息科学专业人士进行了调查。该研究采用内容分析方法，发现虽然 ChatGPT-3 可以成为研究和写作的宝贵工具，但它并不是 100% 准确，应该进行交叉检查。研究还发现，虽然一些院士可能不接受 ChatGPT-3，但大多数院士已经开始接受它。这项研究对院士、内容开发人员和图书馆员都有好处。

AI.tldr: 研究网民、学者及信息专业人士对ChatGPT的看法，分析其作为研究工具的优劣及接受度。
AI.motivation: 探讨AI技术（特别是ChatGPT）在学术领域的应用潜力与挑战，了解不同群体的认知差异。
AI.method: 通过社交媒体评论内容分析及针对信息专业人员的问卷调查法。
AI.result: ChatGPT-3被认可为研究辅助工具但需交叉验证准确性，多数学者态度转向接受。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为学术界、内容开发者及图书馆员提供AI工具使用与管理的参考依据。

authors_text: Bo Zhou,Yu-Jung Tsai,Jiazhen Zhang,Xueqi Guo,Huidong Xie,Xiongchao Chen,Tianshun Miao,Yihuan Lu,James S. Duncan,Chi Liu
title: Fast-MC-PET: A Novel Deep Learning-aided Motion Correction and Reconstruction Framework for Accelerated PET
title_zh: Fast-MC-PET：一种新型深度学习辅助加速 PET 运动校正和重建框架
comment: Accepted at Information Processing in Medical Imaging (IPMI 2023)
comments: Accepted at Information Processing in Medical Imaging (IPMI 2023)
abstract: Patient motion during PET is inevitable. Its long acquisition time not only increases the motion and the associated artifacts but also the patient's discomfort, thus PET acceleration is desirable. However, accelerating PET acquisition will result in reconstructed images with low SNR, and the image quality will still be degraded by motion-induced artifacts. Most of the previous PET motion correction methods are motion type specific that require motion modeling, thus may fail when multiple types of motion present together. Also, those methods are customized for standard long acquisition and could not be directly applied to accelerated PET. To this end, modeling-free universal motion correction reconstruction for accelerated PET is still highly under-explored. In this work, we propose a novel deep learning-aided motion correction and reconstruction framework for accelerated PET, called Fast-MC-PET. Our framework consists of a universal motion correction (UMC) and a short-to-long acquisition reconstruction (SL-Reon) module. The UMC enables modeling-free motion correction by estimating quasi-continuous motion from ultra-short frame reconstructions and using this information for motion-compensated reconstruction. Then, the SL-Recon converts the accelerated UMC image with low counts to a high-quality image with high counts for our final reconstruction output. Our experimental results on human studies show that our Fast-MC-PET can enable 7-fold acceleration and use only 2 minutes acquisition to generate high-quality reconstruction images that outperform/match previous motion correction reconstruction methods using standard 15 minutes long acquisition data.
summary: Patient motion during PET is inevitable. Its long acquisition time not only increases the motion and the associated artifacts but also the patient's discomfort, thus PET acceleration is desirable. However, accelerating PET acquisition will result in reconstructed images with low SNR, and the image quality will still be degraded by motion-induced artifacts. Most of the previous PET motion correction methods are motion type specific that require motion modeling, thus may fail when multiple types of motion present together. Also, those methods are customized for standard long acquisition and could not be directly applied to accelerated PET. To this end, modeling-free universal motion correction reconstruction for accelerated PET is still highly under-explored. In this work, we propose a novel deep learning-aided motion correction and reconstruction framework for accelerated PET, called Fast-MC-PET. Our framework consists of a universal motion correction (UMC) and a short-to-long acquisition reconstruction (SL-Reon) module. The UMC enables modeling-free motion correction by estimating quasi-continuous motion from ultra-short frame reconstructions and using this information for motion-compensated reconstruction. Then, the SL-Recon converts the accelerated UMC image with low counts to a high-quality image with high counts for our final reconstruction output. Our experimental results on human studies show that our Fast-MC-PET can enable 7-fold acceleration and use only 2 minutes acquisition to generate high-quality reconstruction images that outperform/match previous motion correction reconstruction methods using standard 15 minutes long acquisition data.
abstract_zh: PET 期间患者的运动是不可避免的。其较长的采集时间不仅会增加运动和相关伪影，还会增加患者的不适，因此 PET 加速是理想的。然而，加速 PET 采集将导致重建图像的信噪比较低，并且图像质量仍会因运动引起的伪影而降低。以往的PET运动校正方法大多是特定于运动类型的，需要进行运动建模，因此当多种运动类型同时存在时可能会失败。此外，这些方法是为标准长采集定制的，不能直接应用于加速 PET。为此，加速 PET 的免建模通用运动校正重建仍处于高度探索之中。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的深度学习辅助运动校正和加速 PET 重建框架，称为 Fast-MC-PET。我们的框架由通用运动校正（UMC）和短到长采集重建（SL-Reon）模块组成。 UMC 通过从超短帧重建中估计准连续运动并使用此信息进行运动补偿重建，从而实现免建模运动校正。然后，SL-Recon 将低计数的加速 UMC 图像转换为高计数的高质量图像，作为最终的重建输出。我们的人体研究实验结果表明，我们的 Fast-MC-PET 可以实现 7 倍加速，并且仅使用 2 分钟采集即可生成高质量重建图像，该图像优于/匹配以前使用标准 15 分钟长采集数据的运动校正重建方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为Fast-MC-PET的深度学习辅助框架，用于加速正电子发射断层扫描（PET）的运动校正与图像重建。
AI.motivation: PET成像中患者运动会导致图像伪影且加速采集会降低信噪比，传统方法依赖特定运动模型且难以适应加速PET，亟需无需建模的通用解决方案。
AI.method: 框架包含两个模块：1）通用运动校正（UMC）通过超短帧重建估计准连续运动信息；2）短-长采集重建（SL-Recon）将低计数加速图像转换为高质量图像。
AI.result: 实验表明Fast-MC-PET实现7倍加速，仅需2分钟采集即可生成质量优于/等于传统15分钟标准采集的图像。
AI.conclusion: Fast-MC-PET有效解决了加速PET中的运动伪影与图像质量矛盾，为临床快速PET成像提供新思路。

search_text: Fast-MC-PET: A Novel Deep Learning-aided Motion Correction and Reconstruction Framework for Accelerated PET Fast-MC-PET：一种新型深度学习辅助加速 PET 运动校正和重建框架 Patient motion during PET is inevitable. Its long acquisition time not only increases the motion and the associated artifacts but also the patient's discomfort, thus PET acceleration is desirable. However, accelerating PET acquisition will result in reconstructed images with low SNR, and the image quality will still be degraded by motion-induced artifacts. Most of the previous PET motion correction methods are motion type specific that require motion modeling, thus may fail when multiple types of motion present together. Also, those methods are customized for standard long acquisition and could not be directly applied to accelerated PET. To this end, modeling-free universal motion correction reconstruction for accelerated PET is still highly under-explored. In this work, we propose a novel deep learning-aided motion correction and reconstruction framework for accelerated PET, called Fast-MC-PET. Our framework consists of a universal motion correction (UMC) and a short-to-long acquisition reconstruction (SL-Reon) module. The UMC enables modeling-free motion correction by estimating quasi-continuous motion from ultra-short frame reconstructions and using this information for motion-compensated reconstruction. Then, the SL-Recon converts the accelerated UMC image with low counts to a high-quality image with high counts for our final reconstruction output. Our experimental results on human studies show that our Fast-MC-PET can enable 7-fold acceleration and use only 2 minutes acquisition to generate high-quality reconstruction images that outperform/match previous motion correction reconstruction methods using standard 15 minutes long acquisition data. authors_text: Samarth Bhargav,Mohammad Aliannejadi,Evangelos Kanoulas
title: Market-Aware Models for Efficient Cross-Market Recommendation
title_zh: 用于高效跨市场推荐的市场感知模型
abstract: We consider the cross-market recommendation (CMR) task, which involves recommendation in a low-resource target market using data from a richer, auxiliary source market. Prior work in CMR utilised meta-learning to improve recommendation performance in target markets; meta-learning however can be complex and resource intensive. In this paper, we propose market-aware (MA) models, which directly model a market via market embeddings instead of meta-learning across markets. These embeddings transform item representations into market-specific representations. Our experiments highlight the effectiveness and efficiency of MA models both in a pairwise setting with a single target-source market, as well as a global model trained on all markets in unison. In the former pairwise setting, MA models on average outperform market-unaware models in 85% of cases on nDCG@10, while being time-efficient - compared to meta-learning models, MA models require only 15% of the training time. In the global setting, MA models outperform market-unaware models consistently for some markets, while outperforming meta-learning-based methods for all but one market. We conclude that MA models are an efficient and effective alternative to meta-learning, especially in the global setting.
summary: We consider the cross-market recommendation (CMR) task, which involves recommendation in a low-resource target market using data from a richer, auxiliary source market. Prior work in CMR utilised meta-learning to improve recommendation performance in target markets; meta-learning however can be complex and resource intensive. In this paper, we propose market-aware (MA) models, which directly model a market via market embeddings instead of meta-learning across markets. These embeddings transform item representations into market-specific representations. Our experiments highlight the effectiveness and efficiency of MA models both in a pairwise setting with a single target-source market, as well as a global model trained on all markets in unison. In the former pairwise setting, MA models on average outperform market-unaware models in 85% of cases on nDCG@10, while being time-efficient - compared to meta-learning models, MA models require only 15% of the training time. In the global setting, MA models outperform market-unaware models consistently for some markets, while outperforming meta-learning-based methods for all but one market. We conclude that MA models are an efficient and effective alternative to meta-learning, especially in the global setting.
abstract_zh: 我们考虑跨市场推荐（CMR）任务，该任务涉及使用来自更丰富的辅助源市场的数据在资源匮乏的目标市场中进行推荐。 CMR 之前的工作利用元学习来提高目标市场的推荐性能；然而，元学习可能很复杂并且需要大量资源。在本文中，我们提出了市场感知（MA）模型，该模型通过市场嵌入直接对市场进行建模，而不是跨市场的元学习。这些嵌入将项目表示转换为特定于市场的表示。我们的实验强调了 MA 模型在单一目标源市场的配对环境中以及在所有市场上统一训练的全局模型中的有效性和效率。在之前的成对设置中，MA 模型在 nDCG@10 上平均有 85% 的情况优于市场未知模型，同时具有时间效率 - 与元学习模型相比，MA 模型只需要 15% 的训练时间。在全球环境中，MA 模型在某些市场上始终优于市场未知模型，而在除一个市场之外的所有市场上都优于基于元学习的方法。我们的结论是，MA 模型是元学习的高效且有效的替代方案，尤其是在全球环境中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种市场感知模型（MA），用于在低资源目标市场中利用丰富源市场数据进行高效跨市场推荐。
AI.motivation: 现有跨市场推荐方法依赖复杂的元学习，资源消耗大，而直接建模市场差异的更高效方法尚未被充分探索。
AI.method: 通过市场嵌入将物品表示转换为市场特定表示，直接建模市场差异，避免跨市场元学习过程。
AI.result: 在pairwise设置中，MA模型在85%的案例中nDCG@10指标优于无市场感知模型，训练时间仅为元学习的15%；在全局设置中，MA模型在多数市场表现优于无市场感知模型和元学习方法。
AI.conclusion: 市场感知模型是元学习的有效替代方案，尤其在全局跨市场推荐场景中具有显著效率和效果优势。

authors_text: Renliang Sun,Zhixian Yang,Xiaojun Wan
title: Exploiting Summarization Data to Help Text Simplification
title_zh: 利用摘要数据来帮助简化文本
comment: 13 pages, 4 figures, EACL 2023
comments: 13 pages, 4 figures, EACL 2023
abstract: One of the major problems with text simplification is the lack of high-quality data. The sources of simplification datasets are limited to Wikipedia and Newsela, restricting further development of this field. In this paper, we analyzed the similarity between text summarization and text simplification and exploited summarization data to help simplify. First, we proposed an alignment algorithm to extract sentence pairs from summarization datasets. Then, we designed four attributes to characterize the degree of simplification and proposed a method to filter suitable pairs. We named these pairs Sum4Simp (S4S). Next, we conducted human evaluations to show that S4S is high-quality and compared it with a real simplification dataset. Finally, we conducted experiments to illustrate that the S4S can improve the performance of several mainstream simplification models, especially in low-resource scenarios.
summary: One of the major problems with text simplification is the lack of high-quality data. The sources of simplification datasets are limited to Wikipedia and Newsela, restricting further development of this field. In this paper, we analyzed the similarity between text summarization and text simplification and exploited summarization data to help simplify. First, we proposed an alignment algorithm to extract sentence pairs from summarization datasets. Then, we designed four attributes to characterize the degree of simplification and proposed a method to filter suitable pairs. We named these pairs Sum4Simp (S4S). Next, we conducted human evaluations to show that S4S is high-quality and compared it with a real simplification dataset. Finally, we conducted experiments to illustrate that the S4S can improve the performance of several mainstream simplification models, especially in low-resource scenarios.
abstract_zh: 文本简化的主要问题之一是缺乏高质量的数据。简化数据集的来源仅限于Wikipedia和Newsela，限制了该领域的进一步发展。在本文中，我们分析了文本摘要和文本简化之间的相似性，并利用摘要数据来帮助简化。首先，我们提出了一种从摘要数据集中提取句子对的对齐算法。然后，我们设计了四个属性来表征简化程度，并提出了一种过滤合适对的方法。我们将这些对命名为 Sum4Simp (S4S)。接下来，我们进行了人工评估，以表明 S4S 是高质量的，并将其与真实的简化数据集进行了比较。最后，我们进行了实验来说明S4S可以提高几种主流简化模型的性能，特别是在资源匮乏的场景下。

AI.tldr: 本文通过利用文本摘要数据构建高质量文本简化数据集，以解决该领域数据不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有文本简化数据集来源有限，导致模型性能受限，需探索新数据资源以促进发展。
AI.method: 提出对齐算法从摘要数据中提取句子对，设计四维属性筛选简化程度合适的样本，构建Sum4Simp（S4S）数据集。
AI.result: 人工评估证实S4S数据集质量优异，实验表明其可提升主流简化模型性能，尤其在低资源场景下效果显著。
AI.conclusion: 通过跨任务数据复用有效补充了文本简化数据，为模型训练提供了高质量资源，推动领域发展。

search_text: Exploiting Summarization Data to Help Text Simplification 利用摘要数据来帮助简化文本 One of the major problems with text simplification is the lack of high-quality data. The sources of simplification datasets are limited to Wikipedia and Newsela, restricting further development of this field. In this paper, we analyzed the similarity between text summarization and text simplification and exploited summarization data to help simplify. First, we proposed an alignment algorithm to extract sentence pairs from summarization datasets. Then, we designed four attributes to characterize the degree of simplification and proposed a method to filter suitable pairs. We named these pairs Sum4Simp (S4S). Next, we conducted human evaluations to show that S4S is high-quality and compared it with a real simplification dataset. Finally, we conducted experiments to illustrate that the S4S can improve the performance of several mainstream simplification models, especially in low-resource scenarios. 文本简化的主要问题之一是缺乏高质量的数据。简化数据集的来源仅限于Wikipedia和Newsela，限制了该领域的进一步发展。在本文中，我们分析了文本摘要和文本简化之间的相似性，并利用摘要数据来帮助简化。首先，我们提出了一种从摘要数据集中提取句子对的对齐算法。然后，我们设计了四个属性来表征简化程度，并提出了一种过滤合适对的方法。我们将这些对命名为 Sum4Simp (S4S)。接下来，我们进行了人工评估，以表明 S4S 是高质量的，并将其与真实的简化数据集进行了比较。最后，我们进行了实验来说明S4S可以提高几种主流简化模型的性能，特别是在资源匮乏的场景下。 本文通过利用文本摘要数据构建高质量文本简化数据集，以解决该领域数据不足的问题。 Renliang Sun,Zhixian Yang,Xiaojun Wan cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Arpit Bansal,Hong-Min Chu,Avi Schwarzschild,Soumyadip Sengupta,Micah Goldblum,Jonas Geiping,Tom Goldstein
title: Universal Guidance for Diffusion Models
title_zh: 扩散模型通用指南
abstract: Typical diffusion models are trained to accept a particular form of conditioning, most commonly text, and cannot be conditioned on other modalities without retraining. In this work, we propose a universal guidance algorithm that enables diffusion models to be controlled by arbitrary guidance modalities without the need to retrain any use-specific components. We show that our algorithm successfully generates quality images with guidance functions including segmentation, face recognition, object detection, and classifier signals. Code is available at https://github.com/arpitbansal297/Universal-Guided-Diffusion.
summary: Typical diffusion models are trained to accept a particular form of conditioning, most commonly text, and cannot be conditioned on other modalities without retraining. In this work, we propose a universal guidance algorithm that enables diffusion models to be controlled by arbitrary guidance modalities without the need to retrain any use-specific components. We show that our algorithm successfully generates quality images with guidance functions including segmentation, face recognition, object detection, and classifier signals. Code is available at https://github.com/arpitbansal297/Universal-Guided-Diffusion.
abstract_zh: 典型的扩散模型经过训练以接受特定形式的条件作用（最常见的是文本），并且如果不经过重新训练就不能接受其他形式的条件作用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种通用制导算法，该算法使扩散模型能够通过任意制导方式进行控制，而无需重新训练任何特定用途的组件。我们表明，我们的算法成功生成了具有引导功能的高质量图像，包括分割、人脸识别、对象检测和分类器信号。代码可在 https://github.com/arpitbansal297/Universal-Guided-Diffusion 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需重新训练即可控制扩散模型的通用引导算法，使其能够接受多种模态的条件输入。
AI.motivation: 传统扩散模型只能处理特定形式的条件输入（如文本），无法直接通过其他模态（如分割、目标检测等）进行控制，限制了其应用灵活性。
AI.method: 设计了一种通用引导算法，通过将任意模态的指导信号整合到扩散过程中，无需修改模型结构或重新训练组件。
AI.result: 实验表明，该算法能成功生成高质量图像，支持分割、人脸识别、目标检测等多种指导信号的输入。
AI.conclusion: 该方法提供了一种灵活且通用的解决方案，显著提升了扩散模型对多模态条件输入的适应能力。

authors_text: Zhangyang Gao,Yuqi Hu,Cheng Tan,Stan Z. Li
title: PrefixMol: Target- and Chemistry-aware Molecule Design via Prefix Embedding
title_zh: PrefixMol：通过前缀嵌入进行目标和化学感知分子设计
abstract: Is there a unified model for generating molecules considering different conditions, such as binding pockets and chemical properties? Although target-aware generative models have made significant advances in drug design, they do not consider chemistry conditions and cannot guarantee the desired chemical properties. Unfortunately, merging the target-aware and chemical-aware models into a unified model to meet customized requirements may lead to the problem of negative transfer. Inspired by the success of multi-task learning in the NLP area, we use prefix embeddings to provide a novel generative model that considers both the targeted pocket's circumstances and a variety of chemical properties. All conditional information is represented as learnable features, which the generative model subsequently employs as a contextual prompt. Experiments show that our model exhibits good controllability in both single and multi-conditional molecular generation. The controllability enables us to outperform previous structure-based drug design methods. More interestingly, we open up the attention mechanism and reveal coupling relationships between conditions, providing guidance for multi-conditional molecule generation.
summary: Is there a unified model for generating molecules considering different conditions, such as binding pockets and chemical properties? Although target-aware generative models have made significant advances in drug design, they do not consider chemistry conditions and cannot guarantee the desired chemical properties. Unfortunately, merging the target-aware and chemical-aware models into a unified model to meet customized requirements may lead to the problem of negative transfer. Inspired by the success of multi-task learning in the NLP area, we use prefix embeddings to provide a novel generative model that considers both the targeted pocket's circumstances and a variety of chemical properties. All conditional information is represented as learnable features, which the generative model subsequently employs as a contextual prompt. Experiments show that our model exhibits good controllability in both single and multi-conditional molecular generation. The controllability enables us to outperform previous structure-based drug design methods. More interestingly, we open up the attention mechanism and reveal coupling relationships between conditions, providing guidance for multi-conditional molecule generation.
abstract_zh: 是否有一个统一的模型来生成考虑不同条件（例如结合口袋和化学性质）的分子？尽管目标感知生成模型在药物设计方面取得了重大进展，但它们没有考虑化学条件，无法保证所需的化学性质。不幸的是，将目标感知和化学感知模型合并为统一模型以满足定制需求可能会导致负迁移问题。受到 NLP 领域多任务学习成功的启发，我们使用前缀嵌入来提供一种新颖的生成模型，该模型既考虑了目标口袋的情况又考虑了各种化学性质。所有条件信息都表示为可学习的特征，生成模型随后将其用作上下文提示。实验表明，我们的模型在单条件和多条件分子生成中都表现出良好的可控性。这种可控性使我们能够超越以前基于结构的药物设计方法。更有趣的是，我们开放了注意力机制并揭示了条件之间的耦合关系，为多条件分子的生成提供了指导。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PrefixMol模型，通过前缀嵌入技术实现同时考虑靶点信息和化学性质的分子生成。
AI.motivation: 现有药物设计模型或仅关注靶点信息或仅考虑化学性质，难以满足多条件定制需求且存在负迁移风险。
AI.method: 采用自然语言处理中的多任务学习思想，利用前缀嵌入将靶点信息和化学条件编码为可学习特征，作为生成模型的上下文提示。
AI.result: 模型在单条件和多条件分子生成任务中展现优异可控性，性能超越传统结构基药物设计方法，并揭示了条件间耦合关系。
AI.conclusion: PrefixMol通过前缀嵌入有效整合多条件信息，为可控分子生成提供了新范式，其注意力机制分析为多条件设计提供理论指导。

authors_text: Tian Qiu,Linyun Zhou,Wenxiang Xu,Lechao Cheng,Zunlei Feng,Mingli Song
title: Team DETR: Guide Queries as a Professional Team in Detection Transformers
title_zh: DETR 团队：作为检测变压器专业团队指导查询
abstract: Recent proposed DETR variants have made tremendous progress in various scenarios due to their streamlined processes and remarkable performance. However, the learned queries usually explore the global context to generate the final set prediction, resulting in redundant burdens and unfaithful results. More specifically, a query is commonly responsible for objects of different scales and positions, which is a challenge for the query itself, and will cause spatial resource competition among queries. To alleviate this issue, we propose Team DETR, which leverages query collaboration and position constraints to embrace objects of interest more precisely. We also dynamically cater to each query member's prediction preference, offering the query better scale and spatial priors. In addition, the proposed Team DETR is flexible enough to be adapted to other existing DETR variants without increasing parameters and calculations. Extensive experiments on the COCO dataset show that Team DETR achieves remarkable gains, especially for small and large objects. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/horrible-dong/TeamDETR}.
summary: Recent proposed DETR variants have made tremendous progress in various scenarios due to their streamlined processes and remarkable performance. However, the learned queries usually explore the global context to generate the final set prediction, resulting in redundant burdens and unfaithful results. More specifically, a query is commonly responsible for objects of different scales and positions, which is a challenge for the query itself, and will cause spatial resource competition among queries. To alleviate this issue, we propose Team DETR, which leverages query collaboration and position constraints to embrace objects of interest more precisely. We also dynamically cater to each query member's prediction preference, offering the query better scale and spatial priors. In addition, the proposed Team DETR is flexible enough to be adapted to other existing DETR variants without increasing parameters and calculations. Extensive experiments on the COCO dataset show that Team DETR achieves remarkable gains, especially for small and large objects. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/horrible-dong/TeamDETR}.
abstract_zh: 最近提出的 DETR 变体由于其简化的流程和卓越的性能，在各种场景中取得了巨大的进步。然而，学习到的查询通常会探索全局上下文来生成最终的预测集，从而导致冗余负担和不忠实的结果。更具体地说，一个查询通常负责不同尺度和位置的对象，这对查询本身是一个挑战，并且会导致查询之间的空间资源竞争。为了缓解这个问题，我们提出了 Team DETR，它利用查询协作和位置约束来更精确地包含感兴趣的对象。我们还动态地满足每个查询成员的预测偏好，为查询提供更好的规模和空间先验。此外，拟议的 Team DETR 足够灵活，可以适应其他现有的 DETR 变体，而无需增加参数和计算。对 COCO 数据集的大量实验表明，DETR 团队取得了显着的成果，尤其是对于小型和大型物体。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/horrible-dong/TeamDETR} 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Team DETR，通过优化查询协作与位置约束提升检测变压器模型的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有DETR变体中的查询需处理多尺度与多位置目标，导致冗余计算与空间资源竞争，影响检测精度。
AI.method: 设计团队协作机制，为每个查询分配特定空间与尺度先验，减少冗余并增强预测专注度，同时保持参数与计算量不变。
AI.result: 在COCO数据集上显著提升小目标与大目标检测效果，代码已开源。
AI.conclusion: Team DETR通过结构化查询协作有效解决DETR的局限性，为后续模型优化提供新思路。

search_text: Team DETR: Guide Queries as a Professional Team in Detection Transformers DETR 团队：作为检测变压器专业团队指导查询 Recent proposed DETR variants have made tremendous progress in various scenarios due to their streamlined processes and remarkable performance. However, the learned queries usually explore the global context to generate the final set prediction, resulting in redundant burdens and unfaithful results. More specifically, a query is commonly responsible for objects of different scales and positions, which is a challenge for the query itself, and will cause spatial resource competition among queries. To alleviate this issue, we propose Team DETR, which leverages query collaboration and position constraints to embrace objects of interest more precisely. We also dynamically cater to each query member's prediction preference, offering the query better scale and spatial priors. In addition, the proposed Team DETR is flexible enough to be adapted to other existing DETR variants without increasing parameters and calculations. Extensive experiments on the COCO dataset show that Team DETR achieves remarkable gains, especially for small and large objects. Code is available at \url{https://github.com/horrible-dong/TeamDETR}. 最近提出的 DETR 变体由于其简化的流程和卓越的性能，在各种场景中取得了巨大的进步。然而，学习到的查询通常会探索全局上下文来生成最终的预测集，从而导致冗余负担和不忠实的结果。更具体地说，一个查询通常负责不同尺度和位置的对象，这对查询本身是一个挑战，并且会导致查询之间的空间资源竞争。为了缓解这个问题，我们提出了 Team DETR，它利用查询协作和位置约束来更精确地包含感兴趣的对象。我们还动态地满足每个查询成员的预测偏好，为查询提供更好的规模和空间先验。此外，拟议的 Team DETR 足够灵活，可以适应其他现有的 DETR 变体，而无需增加参数和计算。对 COCO 数据集的大量实验表明，DETR 团队取得了显着的成果，尤其是对于小型和大型物体。代码可在 \url{https://github.com/horrible-dong/TeamDETR} 获取。 本文提出Team DETR，通过优化查询协作与位置约束提升检测变压器模型的性能。 Tian Qiu,Linyun Zhou,Wenxiang Xu,Lechao Cheng,Zunlei Feng,Mingli Song cs.CV

authors_text: Antony Thomas,Giulio Ferro,Fulvio Mastrogiovanni,Michela Robba
title: Computational Tradeoff in Minimum Obstacle Displacement Planning for Robot Navigation
title_zh: 机器人导航最小障碍物位移规划的计算权衡
comment: Accepted for presentation at the 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
comments: Accepted for presentation at the 2023 IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation (ICRA)
abstract: In this paper, we look into the minimum obstacle displacement (MOD) planning problem from a mobile robot motion planning perspective. This problem finds an optimal path to goal by displacing movable obstacles when no path exists due to collision with obstacles. However this problem is computationally expensive and grows exponentially in the size of number of movable obstacles. This work looks into approximate solutions that are computationally less intensive and differ from the optimal solution by a factor of the optimal cost.
summary: In this paper, we look into the minimum obstacle displacement (MOD) planning problem from a mobile robot motion planning perspective. This problem finds an optimal path to goal by displacing movable obstacles when no path exists due to collision with obstacles. However this problem is computationally expensive and grows exponentially in the size of number of movable obstacles. This work looks into approximate solutions that are computationally less intensive and differ from the optimal solution by a factor of the optimal cost.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们从移动机器人运动规划的角度研究最小障碍物位移（MOD）规划问题。当由于与障碍物碰撞而不存在路径时，该问题通过移动可移动障碍物来找到到达目标的最佳路径。然而，这个问题的计算成本很高，并且可移动障碍物的数量呈指数级增长。这项工作研究了计算强度较低且与最优解相差一个最优成本的近似解。

AI.tldr: 本文研究机器人导航中在存在障碍物时通过移动障碍物寻找最优路径的问题，并探讨其计算复杂度高的挑战。
AI.motivation: 由于MOD问题的计算复杂度随可移动障碍物数量呈指数增长，现有方法难以高效求解，因此需要寻找计算成本更低的近似解决方案。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种计算成本较低的近似算法，通过允许路径规划结果与最优解存在一定的成本差异，来应对MOD问题的高计算复杂度。
AI.result: 该方法能够在保证路径可行性的同时，显著降低计算成本，且与最优解的偏差控制在可接受的范围内。
AI.conclusion: 通过权衡计算成本与解的最优性，本文提出的近似方法为解决大规模MOD问题提供了可行的解决方案，适用于实际机器人导航中的复杂场景。

authors_text: Xinwei Wang,Zirui Li,Javier Alonso-Mora,Meng Wang
title: Reachability-Based Confidence-Aware Probabilistic Collision Detection in Highway Driving
title_zh: 高速公路驾驶中基于可达性的置信感知概率碰撞检测
comment: Under review at Engineering. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2205.01357
comments: Under review at Engineering. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2205.01357
abstract: Risk assessment is a crucial component of collision warning and avoidance systems in intelligent vehicles. To accurately detect potential vehicle collisions, reachability-based formal approaches have been developed to ensure driving safety, but suffer from over-conservatism, potentially leading to false-positive risk events in complicated real-world applications. In this work, we combine two reachability analysis techniques, i.e., backward reachable set (BRS) and stochastic forward reachable set (FRS), and propose an integrated probabilistic collision detection framework in highway driving. Within the framework, we can firstly use a BRS to formally check whether a two-vehicle interaction is safe; otherwise, a prediction-based stochastic FRS is employed to estimate a collision probability at each future time step. In doing so, the framework can not only identify non-risky events with guaranteed safety, but also provide accurate collision risk estimation in safety-critical events. To construct the stochastic FRS, we develop a neural network-based acceleration model for surrounding vehicles, and further incorporate confidence-aware dynamic belief to improve the prediction accuracy. Extensive experiments are conducted to validate the performance of the acceleration prediction model based on naturalistic highway driving data, and the efficiency and effectiveness of the framework with the infused confidence belief are tested both in naturalistic and simulated highway scenarios. The proposed risk assessment framework is promising in real-world applications.
summary: Risk assessment is a crucial component of collision warning and avoidance systems in intelligent vehicles. To accurately detect potential vehicle collisions, reachability-based formal approaches have been developed to ensure driving safety, but suffer from over-conservatism, potentially leading to false-positive risk events in complicated real-world applications. In this work, we combine two reachability analysis techniques, i.e., backward reachable set (BRS) and stochastic forward reachable set (FRS), and propose an integrated probabilistic collision detection framework in highway driving. Within the framework, we can firstly use a BRS to formally check whether a two-vehicle interaction is safe; otherwise, a prediction-based stochastic FRS is employed to estimate a collision probability at each future time step. In doing so, the framework can not only identify non-risky events with guaranteed safety, but also provide accurate collision risk estimation in safety-critical events. To construct the stochastic FRS, we develop a neural network-based acceleration model for surrounding vehicles, and further incorporate confidence-aware dynamic belief to improve the prediction accuracy. Extensive experiments are conducted to validate the performance of the acceleration prediction model based on naturalistic highway driving data, and the efficiency and effectiveness of the framework with the infused confidence belief are tested both in naturalistic and simulated highway scenarios. The proposed risk assessment framework is promising in real-world applications.
abstract_zh: 风险评估是智能车辆碰撞预警和避免系统的重要组成部分。为了准确检测潜在的车辆碰撞，人们开发了基于可达性的形式化方法来确保驾驶安全，但过于保守，可能导致复杂的现实应用中出现误报风险事件。在这项工作中，我们结合了两种可达性分析技术，即后向可达集（BRS）和随机前向可达集（FRS），并提出了高速公路驾驶中的集成概率碰撞检测框架。在该框架内，我们首先可以使用BRS来形式化地检查两车交互是否安全；否则，采用基于预测的随机 FRS 来估计每个未来时间步的碰撞概率。在此过程中，该框架不仅可以识别保证安全的非风险事件，还可以在安全关键事件中提供准确的碰撞风险估计。为了构建随机 FRS，我们开发了一种基于神经网络的周围车辆加速模型，并进一步结合置信感知动态信念来提高预测精度。进行了大量的实验来验证基于自然高速公路驾驶数据的加速度预测模型的性能，并在自然和模拟高速公路场景中测试了注入置信度的框架的效率和有效性。所提出的风险评估框架在实际应用中很有前景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于可达性分析的置信度感知概率碰撞检测框架，用于高速公路驾驶场景中的风险评估。
AI.motivation: 现有基于可达性的碰撞检测方法存在过度保守问题，导致复杂场景中误报率较高，需提升风险评估的准确性与实用性。
AI.method: 融合逆向可达集（BRS）与随机前向可达集（FRS）技术，结合神经网络加速预测模型及置信度动态信念机制，构建分层碰撞检测框架。
AI.result: 实验表明，该框架在真实高速公路数据及仿真场景中均能有效识别安全事件并准确估计碰撞概率，验证了方法的效率与有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的置信度感知风险评估框架在实际应用中具有潜力，可提升智能车辆碰撞预警系统的安全性和可靠性。
